text.KJV: 1:1 And Iosias helde the Feast of the Passeouer in Ierusalem vnto his Lord, and offered the Passeouer the fourteenth day of the first moneth:
text.NRSV: Josiah kept the passover to his Lord in Jerusalem; he slaughtered the passover lamb on the fourteenth day of the first month,
text.DRB: And Iosias made a Pasch in Jerusalem to our Lord & immolated the Phase the fourteenth moon of the month:
appointing the Priests by courses of days clothed with stoles in the temple of our Lord. 4.
text.KJV: 1:2 Hauing set the Priests according to their daily courses, being arayed in long garments, in the Temple of the Lord.
text.NRSV: having placed the priests according to their divisions, arrayed in their vestments, in the temple of the Lord.

text.DRB: And he spake to the Levites the sacred servants of Israel, that they should sanctify themselves to our Lord in the placing of the holy ark of our Lord in the house, which King Salomon son of David built.
text.KJV: 1:3 And hee spake vnto the Leuites the holy ministers of Israel, that they should hallow themselues vnto the Lord, to set the holy Arke of the Lord, in the house that king Solomon the sonne of Dauid had built:
text.NRSV: He told the Levites, the temple servants of Israel, that they should sanctify themselves to the Lord and put the holy ark of the Lord in the house that King Solomon, son of David, had built;

text.DRB: It shall not be for you to take it upon your shoulders. And now serve your Lord, and take the care of that nation Israel, in part according to your villages & tribes,
text.KJV: 1:4 And said, Ye shall no more beare the Arke vpon your shoulders: now therefore serue the Lord your God, and minister vnto his people Israel, and prepare you after your families and kinreds.
text.NRSV: and he said, ‘You need no longer carry it on your shoulders. Now worship the Lord your God and serve his people Israel; prepare yourselves by your families and kindred,

text.DRB: according to the writing of David king of Israel, and according to the magnificence of Salomon his son, all in the temple, and according to your father's portion of principality, among them that stand in the sight of your brethren the children of Israel.
text.KJV: 1:5 According as Dauid the king of Israel prescribed, & according to the magnificence of Solomon his sonne: & standing in the Temple according to the seuerall dignitie of the families of you the Leuites, who minister in the presence of your brethren the children of Israel.
text.NRSV: in accordance with the directions of King David of Israel and the magnificence of his son Solomon. Stand in order in the temple according to the groupings of the ancestral houses of you Levites, who minister before your kindred the people of Israel,

text.DRB: Immolate the Pasch, and prepare the sacrifices for your brethren, and do according to the precept of our Lord which was given to Moyses.
text.KJV: 1:6 Offer the Passeouer in order, and make ready the sacrifices for your brethren, and keepe the Passeouer according to the commaundement of the Lord, which was giuen vnto Moyses.
text.NRSV: and kill the passover lamb and prepare the sacrifices for your kindred, and keep the passover according to the commandment of the Lord that was given to Moses.’

text.DRB: And Iosias gave unto the people that was found of sheep, lambs, and kids, & goats thirty thousand, calves three thousand. . . .
text.KJV: 1:7 And vnto the people that was found there, Iosias gaue thirtie thousand lambes, and kids, and three thousand calues: these things were giuen of the kings allowance, according as hee promised to the people, to the Priestes, and to the Leuites.
text.NRSV: To the people who were present Josiah gave thirty thousand lambs and kids, and three thousand calves; these were given from the king’s possessions, as he promised, to the people and the priests and Levites.

text.DRB: These things were given to the people^ of the kings' goods according to promise: and to the Priests for the Phase, sheep in number two thousand, and calves an hundred.
text.KJV: 1:8 And Helkias, Zacharias, and Sielus the gouernours of the Temple, gaue to the Priests for the Passeouer, two thousand and sixe hundred sheepe, and three hundreth calues.
text.NRSV: Hilkiah, Zechariah, and Jehiel,] the chief officers of the temple, gave to the priests for the passover two thousand six hundred sheep and three hundred calves.

text.DRB: And Jechonias, and Semeias, and Nathanael brethren, and Hasabias, and Oziel, & Coraba, for the Phase sheep five thousand, calves five hundred.
text.KJV: 1:9 And Iechonias, and Samaias, and Nathanael his brother, and Assabias, and Ochiel, and Ioram captaines ouer thousands, gaue to the Leuites for the Passeouer fiue thousand sheepe, and seuen hundreth calues.
text.NRSV: And Jeconiah and Shemaiah and his brother Nethanel, and Hashabiah and Ochiel and Joram, captains over thousands, gave the Levites for the passover five thousand sheep and seven hundred calves.

text.DRB: And when these things were done in good order, the Priests and the Levites stood having azymes by tribes.
text.KJV: 1:10 And when these things were done, the Priests and Leuites hauing the vnleauened bread, stood in very comely order according to the kinreds,
text.NRSV: This is what took place. The priests and the Levites, having the unleavened bread, stood in proper order, according to kindred

text.DRB: And according to the portions of their fathers' principality, in the sight of the people they did offer to our Lord according to those things, which were written in the book of Moyses:
text.KJV: 1:11 And according to the seuerall dignities of the fathers, before the people, to offer to the Lord, as it is written in the booke of Moyses: And thus did they in the morning.
text.NRSV: and the grouping of the ancestral houses, before the people to make the offering to the Lord as it is written in the book of Moses; this they did in the morning.

text.DRB: and roasted the Phase with fire as it ought: and the hosts they boiled in cauldrons, and in pots with benevolence:
text.KJV: 1:12 And they rosted the Passeouer with fire, as appertaineth: as for the sacrifices, they sodde them in brasse pots, and pannes with a good sauour.
text.NRSV: They roasted the passover lamb with fire, as required; and they boiled the sacrifices in bronze pots and cauldrons, with a pleasing odour,

text.DRB: and they brought to all that were of the people: and afterward they prepared for themselves and the
text.KJV: 1:13 And set them before all the people, and afterward they prepared for themselues, and for the Priests their brethren the sonnes of Aaron.
text.NRSV: and carried them to all the people. Afterwards they prepared the passover for themselves and for their kindred the priests, the sons of Aaron,

text.DRB: For the Priests offered the fat, until the hour was ended: & the Levites prepared for themselves, and their brethren, the children of Aaron.
text.KJV: 1:14 For the Priests offered the fat vntill night: and the Leuites prepared for themselues, and the Priests their brethren the sonnes of Aaron.
text.NRSV: because the priests were offering the fat until nightfall; so the Levites prepared it for themselves and for their kindred the priests, the sons of Aaron.

text.DRB: And the sacred singing men, the children of Asaph were by order according to the precept of David, & Asaph, & Zacharias, & Ieddimus, which was from the king.
text.KJV: 1:15 The holy Singers also, the sonnes of Asaph, were in their order, according to the appointment of Dauid, to wit, Asaph, Zacharias, and Ieduthun, who was of the kings retinue.
text.NRSV: The temple singers, the sons of Asaph, were in their place according to the arrangement made by David, and also Asaph, Zechariah, and Eddinus, who represented the king.

text.DRB: And the porters at every gate, so that none transgressed his own: for their brethren prepared for them.
text.KJV: 1:16 Moreouer the porters were at euery gate: it was not lawfull for any to goe from his ordinary seruice: for their brethren the Leuites prepared for them.
text.NRSV: The gatekeepers were at each gate; no one needed to interrupt his daily duties, for their kindred the Levites prepared the passover for them.

text.DRB: And the things were consummate that pertained to the sacrifice of our Lord.
text.KJV: 1:17 Thus were the things that belonged to the sacrifices of the Lord accomplished in that day, that they might hold the Passeouer,
text.NRSV: So the things that had to do with the sacrifices to the Lord were accomplished that day: the passover was kept

text.DRB: In that day they celebrated the Phase, and offered hosts upon the sacrifice of our Lord, according to the precept of king Iosias.
text.KJV: 1:18 And offer sacrifices vpon the altar of the Lord, according to the commandement of king Iosias.
text.NRSV: and the sacrifices were offered on the altar of the Lord, according to the command of King Josiah.

text.DRB: And the children of Israel, that were found at that time, celebrated the Phase: and the festival day of Azymes for seven days:
text.KJV: 1:19 So the children of Israel which were present, held the Passeouer at that time, and the feast of sweet bread seuen dayes.
text.NRSV: And the people of Israel who were present at that time kept the passover and the festival of unleavened bread for seven days.

text.DRB: and there was not celebrated such a Phase in Israel, from the times of Samuel the Prophet:
text.KJV: 1:20 And such a Passeouer was not kept in Israel since the time of the Prophet Samuel.
text.NRSV: No passover like it had been kept in Israel since the times of the prophet Samuel;

text.DRB: and all the kings of Israel did not celebrate such a Phase as Iosias did, and the Priests, and the Levites, and the Jews, and all Israel, that were found in their abode at Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 1:21 Yea all the kings of Israel held not such a Passeouer as Iosias, and the Priests and the Leuites, & the Iewes held with all Israel that were found dwelling at Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: none of the kings of Israel had kept such a passover as was kept by Josiah and the priests and Levites and the people of Judah and all of Israel who were living in Jerusalem.

text.DRB: In the eighteenth year, Iosias reigning, was the Phase celebrated.
text.KJV: 1:22 In the eighteenth yeere of the reigne of Iosias was this Passeouer kept.
text.NRSV: In the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah this passover was kept.

text.KJV: 1:23 And the workes of Iosias were vpright before his Lord with an heart full of godlinesse.
text.NRSV: The events of Josiah’s reign had been right in the sight of his Lord, for his heart was full of godliness.
text.DRB: And the works of Iosias were directed in the sight of his Lord in a heart full of fear:

and the things concerning him are written in the ancient times, touching them that sinned, and were irreligious against our Lord above all nations, and that sought not the words of our Lord upon Israel.
text.KJV: 1:24 As for the things that came to passe in his time, they were written in former times, concerning those that sinned, and did wickedly against the Lord aboue all people and kingdomes, and how they grieued him exceedingly, so that the words of the Lord rose vp against Israel.
text.NRSV: In ancient times the events of his reign have been recorded—concerning those who sinned and acted wickedly towards the Lord beyond any other people or kingdom, and how they grieved the Lord] deeply, so that the words of the Lord fell upon Israel.

text.DRB: And after all this fact of Iosias, came up Pharao the king of Ægypt coming in Charcamis from the way upon Euphrates, and Iosias went forth to meet him. 4.
text.KJV: 1:25 Now after all these acts of Iosias, it came to passe that Pharao the king of Egypt came to raise warre at Carchamis vpon Euphrates: and Iosias went out against him.
text.NRSV: After all these acts of Josiah, it happened that Pharaoh, king of Egypt, went to make war at Carchemish on the Euphrates, and Josiah went out against him.

text.DRB: And the king of Ægypt sent to Iosias saying: What is there between me & thee king of Juda?
text.KJV: 1:26 But the king of Egypt sent to him saying, What haue I to doe with thee, O king of Iudea?
text.NRSV: And the king of Egypt sent word to him saying, ‘What have we to do with each other, O king of Judea?

text.DRB: I was not sent of the Lord to fight against thee: for my battle is upon Euphrates, go down in haste.
text.KJV: 1:27 I am not sent out from the Lord God against thee: for my warre is vpon Euphrates, and now the Lord is with mee, yea the Lord is with mee hasting me forward: Depart from me and be not against the Lord.
text.NRSV: I was not sent against you by the Lord God, for my war is at the Euphrates. And now the Lord is with me! The Lord is with me, urging me on! Stand aside, and do not oppose the Lord.’

text.DRB: And Iosias did not return upon his chariot: but endeavoured to overthrow him, not attending the word of the Prophet from the mouth of our Lord:
text.KJV: 1:28 Howbeit Iosias did not turne backe his chariot from him, but vndertooke to fight with him, not regarding the words of the Prophet Ieremie, spoken by the mouth of the Lord:
text.NRSV: Josiah, however, did not turn back to his chariot, but tried to fight with him, and did not heed the words of the prophet Jeremiah from the mouth of the Lord.

text.DRB: but he made battle against him in the field of Mageddo. And Princes went down to king Iosias.
text.KJV: 1:29 But ioyned battell with him in the plaine of Magiddo, and the princes came against king Iosias.
text.NRSV: He joined battle with him in the plain of Megiddo, and the commanders came down against King Josiah.

text.DRB: And the king said to his servants: Remove me from the battle, for I am weakened exceedingly. And forthwith his servants removed him out of the battle.
text.KJV: 1:30 Then said the king vnto his seruants, carry me away out of the battell for I am very weake: and immediately his seruants tooke him away out of the battell.
text.NRSV: The king said to his servants, ‘Take me away from the battle, for I am very weak.’ And immediately his servants took him out of the line of battle.

text.DRB: And he went up into his second chariot: & coming to Jerusalem, died, and was buried in his father's sepulchre.
text.KJV: 1:31 Then gate he vp vpon his second chariot, and being brought backe to Ierusalem, dyed, and was buried in his fathers sepulchre.
text.NRSV: He got into his second chariot; and after he was brought back to Jerusalem he died, and was buried in the tomb of his ancestors.

text.KJV: 1:32 And in all Iury they mourned for Iosias, yea Ieremie the Prophet lamented for Iosias, and the cheefe men with the women made lamentation for him vnto this day: and this was giuen out for an ordinance to be done continually in all the nation of Israel.
text.NRSV: Josiah died, and was buried in the tomb of his ancestors in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And in all Jury they mourned for Iosias, & the rulers with their wives lamented him until this day. And this was given out to be done always unto all the stock of Israel.

But these things were written before in the book of the histories of the kings of Juda: and all the acts of the doing of Iosias, and his glory and his understanding in the law of our Lord: and the things that were done by him, and that are not written in the book of the kings of Israel and Juda.
text.KJV: 1:33 These things are written in the booke of the stories of the kings of Iudah, and euery one of the acts that Iosias did, and his glory, and his vnderstanding in the law of the Lord, and the things that he had done before, and the things now recited, are reported in the bookes of the Kings of Israel and Iudea.
text.NRSV: These things are written in the book of the histories of the kings of Judea; and every one of the acts of Josiah, and his splendour, and his understanding of the law of the Lord, and the things that he had done before, and these that are now told, are recorded in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah.

text.DRB: And they that were of the nation, taking Jechonias the son of Iosias, made him king for Iosias his father, when he was three and twenty years old. 4. . v. 30. 2. Par. 36. v. 1.
text.KJV: 1:34 And the people tooke Ioachaz the sonne of Iosias, and made him king in stead of Iosias his father, when hee was twentie and three yeeres old.
text.NRSV: The men of the nation took Jeconiah] son of Josiah, who was twenty-three years old, and made him king in succession to his father Josiah.

text.DRB: And he reigned over Israel three months. And the king of Ægypt removed him, that he should not reign in Jerusalem:
text.KJV: 1:35 And he reigned in Iudea and in Ierusalem three moneths: and then the King of Egypt deposed him from reigning in Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: He reigned for three months in Judah and Jerusalem. Then the king of Egypt deposed him from reigning in Jerusalem,

text.DRB: & he put a tax upon the nation^ of silver an hundred talents, and of gold one talent.
text.KJV: 1:36 And he set a taxe vpon the land of an hundreth talents of siluer, and one talent of gold.
text.NRSV: and fined the nation one hundred talents of silver and one talent of gold.

text.DRB: And the king of Ægypt made Ioacim his brother king of Juda & Jerusalem:
text.KJV: 1:37 The king of Egypt also made king Ioacim his brother king of Iudea and Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: The king of Egypt made his brother Jehoiakim king of Judea and Jerusalem.

text.DRB: & he bound the magistrates of Ioacim, and Zaracel his brother, and taking them brought them back into Ægypt.
text.KJV: 1:38 And hee bound Ioacim and the nobles: but Zaraces his brother he apprehended, and brought him out of Egypt.
text.NRSV: Jehoiakim put the nobles in prison, and seized his brother Zarius and brought him back from Egypt.

text.DRB: Ioacim was five and twenty years old when he began to reign in the land of Juda and Jerusalem: & he did evil in the sight of our Lord.
text.KJV: 1:39 Fiue and twentie yeere old was Ioacim when he was made king in the land of Iudea and Ierusalem, and he did euill before the Lord.
text.NRSV: Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign in Judea and Jerusalem; he did what was evil in the sight of the Lord.

text.DRB: And after this man came up Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon, and binding him with a band of brass, brought him into Babylon.
text.KJV: 1:40 Wherefore against him Nabuchodonosor the King of Babylon came vp, and bound him with a chaine of brasse, and carried him vnto Babylon.
text.NRSV: King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came up against him; he bound him with a chain of bronze and took him away to Babylon.

text.DRB: And Nabuchodonosor took the sacred vessels of our Lord, and carried away, and consecrated them in his temple in Babylon.
text.KJV: 1:41 Nabuchodonosor also tooke of the holy vessels of the Lord, and carried them away, and set them in his owne temple at Babylon.
text.NRSV: Nebuchadnezzar also took some holy vessels of the Lord, and carried them away, and stored them in his temple in Babylon.

text.DRB: For his uncleanness, & lack of religion is written in the book of the times of the kings.
text.KJV: 1:42 But those things that are recorded of him, and of his vncleannes, and impietie, are written in the Chronicles of the kings.
text.NRSV: But the things that are reported about Jehoiakim,] and his uncleanness and impiety, are written in the annals of the kings.

text.DRB: And Ioachin his son reigned for him. And when he was made king, he was eighteen years old.
text.KJV: 1:43 And Ioacim his sonne reigned in his stead: he was made king being eighteene yeeres old,
text.NRSV: His son Jehoiachin] became king in his place; when he was made king he was eighteen years old,

text.DRB: And reigned three months and ten days in Jerusalem, and did evil in the sight of our Lord:
text.KJV: 1:44 And reigned but three moneths and ten dayes in Ierusalem, and did euill before the Lord.
text.NRSV: and he reigned for three months and ten days in Jerusalem. He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord.

text.DRB: and after a year Nabuchodonosor sending, transported him into Babylon together with the sacred vessels of our Lord.
text.KJV: 1:45 So after a yere Nabuchodonosor sent, and caused him to be brought into Babylon with þ holy vessels of þ Lord,
text.NRSV: A year later Nebuchadnezzar sent and removed him to Babylon, with the holy vessels of the Lord,

text.DRB: And he made Sedecias king of Juda and
text.KJV: 1:46 And made Zedechias king of Iudea and Ierusalem, when he was one and twentie yeeres old, and he reigned eleuen yeeres:
text.NRSV: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old, and he reigned for eleven years.

text.DRB: And he did evil in the sight of our Lord, and was not afraid of the words which were spoken by Jeremy the Prophet from the mouth of our Lord:
text.KJV: 1:47 And he did euill also in the sight of the Lord, & cared not for the words that were spoken vnto him, by the Prophet Ieremie from the mouth of the Lord.
text.NRSV: He also did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, and did not heed the words that were spoken by the prophet Jeremiah from the mouth of the Lord.

text.DRB: and being sworn of king Nabuchodonosor, forsworn he did revolt: & his neck being hardened, & his heart, he transgressed the ordinances of our Lord the God of Israel.
text.KJV: 1:48 And after that king Nabuchodonosor had made him to sweare by the Name of the Lord, he forswore himselfe, and rebelled, and hardening his necke, and his heart, hee transgressed the lawes of the Lord God of Israel.
text.NRSV: Although King Nebuchadnezzar had made him swear by the name of the Lord, he broke his oath and rebelled; he stiffened his neck and hardened his heart and transgressed the laws of the Lord, the God of Israel.

text.DRB: And the Princes of the people of our Lord did many things wickedly, and they did impiously above all the uncleanness of the nations: & they polluted the temple of our Lord that was holy in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 1:49 The gouernours also of the people and of the priests did many things against the lawes, and passed al the pollutions of all nations, and defiled the Temple of the Lord which was sanctified in Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: Even the leaders of the people and of the priests committed many acts of sacrilege and lawlessness beyond all the unclean deeds of all the nations, and polluted the temple of the Lord in Jerusalem—the temple that God had made holy.

text.DRB: And the God of their fathers sent by his messenger to reclaim them, for that he would spare them, and his tabernacle.
text.KJV: 1:50 Neuerthelesse, the God of their fathers sent by his messenger to call them backe, because he spared them and his tabernacle also:
text.NRSV: The God of their ancestors sent his messenger to call them back, because he wished to spare them and his dwelling-place.

text.DRB: But they scorned at his messengers: and in the day that our Lord spake to them they were mocking his Prophets.
text.KJV: 1:51 But they had his messengers in derision, and looke when the Lorde spake vnto them, they made a sport of his prophets,
text.NRSV: But they mocked his messengers, and whenever the Lord spoke, they scoffed at his prophets,

text.DRB: Who was moved even unto wrath upon his nation for their impiety, and commanded the kings of the Chaldees to come up.
text.KJV: 1:52 So farre foorth that he being wroth with his people for their great vngodlinesse, commanded the kings of the Caldees to come vp against them.
text.NRSV: until in his anger against his people because of their ungodly acts he gave command to bring against them the kings of the Chaldeans.

text.KJV: 1:53 Who flew their yong men with the sword, yea euen within the compasse of their holy Temple, & spared neither yong man nor maid, old man nor child among them, for hee deliuered all into their hands.
text.NRSV: The Chaldeans killed their young men with the sword around their holy temple; they did not spare young man or young woman, old man or child, for he gave them all into their hands.
text.DRB: These slew their young men with the sword, round about their holy temple, and spared not young man, and old man, and Virgin, and youth:
but all were delivered into their hands: & taking all the sacred vessels of our Lord, and the kings' treasures, they carried them into Babylon,
text.KJV: 1:54 And they tooke all the holy vessels of the Lord, both great and small, with the vessels of the Ark of God, and the kings treasures, and caried them away into Babylon.
text.NRSV: They took all the holy vessels of the Lord, great and small, the treasure-chests of the Lord, and the royal stores, and carried them away to Babylon.

text.KJV: 1:55 As for the house of the Lord they burnt it, brake downe the walles of Ierusalem, set fire vpon her towres.
text.NRSV: They burned the house of the Lord and broke down the walls of Jerusalem; they burned their towers with fire.
text.DRB: and burnt the house of our Lord, and threw down the walls of Jerusalem: and the towers thereof they burnt with fire,
and consumed all their honourable things, and brought them to naught, and those that were left of the sword, they led into Babylon.
text.KJV: 1:56 And as for her glorious things, they neuer ceased til they had consumed and brought them all to nought, and the people that were not slaine with the sword, he caried vnto Babylon:
text.NRSV: and utterly destroyed all its glorious things. The survivors he led away to Babylon with the sword,

text.DRB: And they were his servants until the Persians reigned in the fulfilling of the word of our Lord by the mouth of Jeremy: . v. 12. & 29. v.
text.KJV: 1:57 Who became seruants to him and his children, till the Persians reigned, to fulfill the word of the Lord spoken by the mouth of Ieremie:
text.NRSV: and they were servants to him and to his sons until the Persians began to reign, in fulfilment of the word of the Lord by the mouth of Jeremiah,

text.DRB: as long as the land quietly kept her sabbaths, all the time of her desolation she sabbathized in the application of seventy years.
text.KJV: 1:58 Untill the land had enioyed her Sabbaths, the whole time of her desolation shal she rest, vntill the full terme of seuentie yeeres.
text.NRSV: saying, ‘Until the land has enjoyed its sabbaths, it shall keep sabbath all the time of its desolation until the completion of seventy years.’

text.KJV: 2:1 In the first yeere of Cyrus king of the Persians, that the worde of the Lorde might bee accomplished, that hee had promised by the mouth of Ieremie:
text.NRSV: In the first year of Cyrus king of the Persians, so that the word of the Lord by the mouth of Jeremiah might be accomplished,
text.DRB: Cyrus king of the Persians reigning for the accomplishment of the word of our Lord by the mouth of Jeremy, 2. . v. 22. 2. . v. 1. & 6. v. 3. . v. 12. & 29. v.

text.KJV: 2:2 The Lord raised vp the spirit of Cyrus the king of the Persians, and he made proclamation thorow al his kingdome, and also by writing,
text.NRSV: the Lord stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of the Persians, and he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom and also put it in writing:
text.DRB: our Lord raised up the spirit of Cyrus King of the Persians, and he proclaimed in all his Kingdoms, and that by writing,

text.KJV: 2:3 Saying, Thus saith Cyrus king of the Persians, The Lord of Israel the most high Lord, hath made me king of the whole world,
text.NRSV: “Thus says Cyrus king of the Persians: The Lord of Israel, the Lord Most High, has made me king of the world,”
text.DRB: saying: Thus saith Cyrus king of the Persians: The Lord of Israel, the high Lord, hath made me king over the whole earth,

text.KJV: 2:4 And commanded me to build him an house at Ierusalem in Iurie.
text.NRSV: “and he has commanded me to build him a house at Jerusalem, which is in Judea.”
text.DRB: & hath signified to me to build him a house in Jerusalem which is in Jury.
If there be any of your kindred, his Lord go up with him into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 2:5 If therefore there bee any of you that are of his people, let the Lord, euen his Lord be with him, and let him goe vp to Ierusalem that is in Iudea, and build the house of the Lord of Israel: for he is the Lord that dwelleth in Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: If any of you, therefore, are of his people, may your Lord be with you; go up to Jerusalem, which is in Judea, and build the house of the Lord of Israel—he is the Lord who dwells in Jerusalem—

text.DRB: Whosoever therefore dwell about the places, let them help them that are in the same place, in gold & silver,
text.KJV: 2:6 Whosoeuer then dwell in the places about, let them helpe him, those I say that are his neighbours, with gold and with siluer,
text.NRSV: and let each of you, wherever you may live, be helped by the people of your place with gold and silver,

text.DRB: in gifts, with horses, and beasts, and with other things which by vows are added into the temple of our Lord which is in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 2:7 With gifts, with horses, and with cattell, and other things, which haue bene set forth by vowe, for the Temple of the Lord at Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: with gifts and with horses and cattle, besides the other things added as votive offerings for the temple of the Lord that is in Jerusalem.’

text.DRB: And the Princes of the tribes, of the villages, and of Jury, of the tribe of Benjamin, & the Priests, and the Levites standing up, whom our Lord moved to go up, and to build the house of our Lord which is in Jerusalem, and they that were round about them,
text.KJV: 2:8 ¶ Then the chiefe of the families of Iudea, and of the tribes of Beniamin stood vp: the priests also and the Leuites, and all they whose minde the Lord had moued to goe vp, and to build an house for the Lord at Ierusalem,
text.NRSV: Then arose the heads of families of the tribes of Judah and Benjamin, and the priests and the Levites, and all whose spirit the Lord had stirred to go up to build the house in Jerusalem for the Lord;

text.DRB: did help them with all their gold and silver, and beasts, and many whose mind was stirred up, with many vows.
text.KJV: 2:9 And they that dwelt round about them, and helped them in all things with siluer and gold, with horses and cattell, and with very free gifts of a great number whose mindes were stirred vp thereto.
text.NRSV: their neighbours helped them with everything, with silver and gold, with horses and cattle, and with a very great number of votive offerings from many whose hearts were stirred.

text.DRB: And Cyrus the king brought forth the sacred vessels of our Lord, which Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon transported out of Jerusalem, & consecrated them to his Idol.
text.KJV: 2:10 King Cyrus also brought foorth the holy vessels which Nabuchodonosor had caried away from Ierusalem, and had set vp in his temple of idoles.
text.NRSV: King Cyrus also brought out the holy vessels of the Lord that Nebuchadnezzar had carried away from Jerusalem and stored in his temple of idols.

text.DRB: And Cyrus the king of Persians bringing them forth, delivered them to Mithridatus, who was over his treasures.
text.KJV: 2:11 Now when Cyrus king of the Persians had brought them foorth, hee deliuered them to Mithridates his treasurer:
text.NRSV: When King Cyrus of the Persians brought these out, he gave them to Mithridates, his treasurer,

text.DRB: And by him they were delivered to Salmanasar president of Jury.
text.KJV: 2:12 And by him they were deliuered to Sanabassar þ gouernour of Iudea.
text.NRSV: and by him they were given to Sheshbazzar,] the governor of Judea.

text.DRB: And of these this was the number: Cups for
text.KJV: 2:13 And this was the number of them, a thousand golden cuppes, and a thousand of siluer, censers of siluer twentie nine, vials of gold thirtie, and of siluer two thousand foure hundred and ten, and a thousand other vessels.
text.NRSV: The number of these was: one thousand gold cups, one thousand silver cups, twenty-nine silver censers, thirty gold bowls, two thousand four hundred and ten silver bowls, and one thousand other vessels.

text.DRB: & all the vessels of gold & silver, were five thousand eight hundred sixty.
text.KJV: 2:14 So all the vessels of gold, and of siluer which were caried away, were fiue thousand, foure hundred, threescore and nine.
text.NRSV: All the vessels were handed over, gold and silver, five thousand four hundred and sixty-nine,

text.DRB: And they were numbered to Salmanasar together with them that came out of the captivity of Babylon into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 2:15 These were brought back by Sanabassar, together with them of the captiuity, from Babylon to Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: and they were carried back by Sheshbazzar with the returning exiles from Babylon to Jerusalem.

text.DRB: But in the times of Artaxerxes king of the Persians, there wrote to him of them that dwelt in Jury and Jerusalem, Balsamus, and Mithridatus, and Sabellius, and Rathimus, Balthemus, Sabellius scribe, and the rest dwelling in Samaria, and other places, the epistle following to king Artaxerxes.
text.KJV: 2:16 But in the time of Artaxerxes king of the Persians, Belemus, and Mithridates, and Tabellius, and Rathumus, and Beeltethmus, and Semellius the Secretarie, with others that were in commission with them, dwelling in Samaria and other places, wrote vnto him against them that dwelt in Iudea and Ierusalem, these letters following.
text.NRSV: In the time of King Artaxerxes of the Persians, Bishlam, Mithridates, Tabeel, Rehum, Beltethmus, the scribe Shimshai, and the rest of their associates, living in Samaria and other places, wrote him the following letter, against those who were living in Judea and Jerusalem:

text.DRB: SIR, thy servants Rathimus over occurrents, and Sabellius the scribe, and the other judges of thy court in Cælesyria, and Phenice.
text.KJV: 2:17 To King Artaxerxes our lord, Thy seruants Rathumus the story writer, and Semellius the scribe, and the rest of their counsell, and the Iudges that are in Coelosyria and Phenice.
text.NRSV: ‘To King Artaxerxes our lord, your servants the recorder Rehum and the scribe Shimshai and the other members of their council, and the judges in Coelesyria and Phoenicia:

text.DRB: And now be it known to our lord the king, that Jews came up from you to us, coming into Jerusalem a rebellious, & very naughty city, do build the furnaces thereof, and set up the walls, and raise the temple.
text.KJV: 2:18 Be it now knowen to the lord the king, that the Iewes that are come vp from you to vs, being come into Ierusalem (that rebellious and wicked citie,) doe build the market places, and repaire the walles of it, and doe lay the foundation of the Temple.
text.NRSV: Let it now be known to our lord the king that the Jews who came up from you to us have gone to Jerusalem and are building that rebellious and wicked city, repairing its market-places and walls and laying the foundations for a temple.

text.DRB: And if this city, and the walls shall be finished, they will not only not abide to pay tributes, but also will resist the kings.
text.KJV: 2:19 Now if this citie, and the walles thereof be made vp againe, they will not onely refuse to giue tribute, but also rebell against kings.
text.NRSV: Now if this city is built and the walls finished, they will not only refuse to pay tribute but will even resist kings.

text.DRB: And because that is in doing about the temple, we thought it should do well not to neglect this same thing:
text.KJV: 2:20 And forasmuch as the things pertaining to the Temple, are now in hand, we thinke it meete not to neglect such a matter,
text.NRSV: Since the building of the temple is now going on, we think it best not to neglect such a matter,

text.DRB: but to make it known to our lord the king, that if it shall seem good, o king, it may be sought in the books of thy fathers,
text.KJV: 2:21 But to speake vnto our lord the king, to the intent that if it be thy pleasure, it may be sought out in the bookes of thy fathers:
text.NRSV: but to speak to our lord the king, in order that, if it seems good to you, search may be made in the records of your ancestors.

text.DRB: & thou shalt find in the records, things written of these, and thou shalt know that this city hath been rebellious, & troubling kings, & cities,
text.KJV: 2:22 And thou shalt finde in the Chronicles, what is written concerning these things, and shalt vnderstand that that citie was rebellious, troubling both kings and cities:
text.NRSV: You will find in the annals what has been written about them, and will learn that this city was rebellious, troubling both kings and other cities,

text.DRB: and the Jews rebels, & making battles in it from time out of mind, for the which cause this city was made desolate.
text.KJV: 2:23 And that the Iewes were rebellious, and raised alwayes warres therin, for the which cause euen this citie was made desolate.
text.NRSV: and that the Jews were rebels and kept setting up blockades in it from of old. That is why this city was laid waste.

text.DRB: Now therefore we do thee to understand, lord king, that if this city shall be built, & the walls thereof shall be erected, there will be no coming down for thee into Cælesyria & Phenice.
text.KJV: 2:24 Wherefore now wee doe declare vnto thee, (O lord the king) that if this citie bee built againe, and the walles thereof set vp anew, thou shalt from hencefoorth haue no passage into Corlosyria and Phenice.
text.NRSV: Therefore we now make known to you, O lord and king, that if this city is built and its walls finished, you will no longer have access to Coelesyria and Phoenicia.’

text.DRB: Then wrote the king to Rathimus, the writer of the occurrents, and to Balthemus, and to Sabellius the scribe, and to the rest joined with them, and to the dwellers in Syria and Phenice, as followeth:
text.KJV: 2:25 Then the King wrote backe againe to Rathumus the storie-writer, to Beeltethmus, to Semellius the scribe, and to the rest that were in commission, and dwellers in Samaria and Syria, and Phenice, after this maner.
text.NRSV: Then the king, in reply to the recorder Rehum, Beltethmus, the scribe Shimshai, and the others associated with them and living in Samaria and Syria and Phoenicia, wrote as follows:

text.DRB: I have read the epistle that you sent me. I commanded therefore search to be made, & it was found that the same city is from the beginning rebellious to kings,
text.KJV: 2:26 I haue read the Epistle which ye haue sent vnto mee: therefore I commanded to make diligent search, and it hath bene found, that that city was from the beginning practising against Kings.
text.NRSV: ‘I have read the letter that you sent me. So I ordered search to be made, and it has been found that this city from of old has fought against kings,

text.DRB: and the men rebels, and making battles in it, & there were most valiant kings ruling in Jerusalem, and exacting tributes in Cælesyria & Phenice.
text.KJV: 2:27 And the men therein were giuen to rebellion, and warre, and that mightie Kings and fierce were in Ierusalem, who reigned and exacted tributes in Coelosyria and Phenice.
text.NRSV: that the people in it were given to rebellion and war, and that mighty and cruel kings ruled in Jerusalem and exacted tribute from Coelesyria and Phoenicia.

text.DRB: Now therefore I have given commandment to forbid those men to build the city, and to stay them that nothing be done more than is:
text.KJV: 2:28 Now therefore I haue commanded to hinder those men from building the citie, and heed to be taken that there be no more done in it,
text.NRSV: Therefore I have now issued orders to prevent these people from building the city and to take care that nothing more be done

text.DRB: and that they proceed not farther, whereof are evils, so that there may be trouble brought upon the kings.
text.KJV: 2:29 And that those wicked workers proceed no further to the annoyance of Kings.
text.NRSV: and that such wicked proceedings go no further to the annoyance of kings.’

text.KJV: 2:30 Then king Artaxerxes his letters being read, Rathumus and Semellius the scribe, and the rest that were in commission with them, remoouing in hast towards Ierusalem with a troupe of horsemen, and a multitude of people in battell aray, began to hinder the builders, and the building of the Temple in Ierusalem ceased vntill the second yeere of the reigne of Darius King of the Persians.
text.NRSV: Then the building of the temple in Jerusalem stopped, and was discontinued until the second year of the reign of Darius king of the Persians.
text.DRB: Then these things being read which were written of king Artaxerxes, Rathimus, and Sabellius the scribe, and they that were appointed with them joining together in haste came to Jerusalem with a troop of horsemen, and multitude, & company:
and they began to forbid the builders, and they ceased from building of the temple in Jerusalem, till in the second year of the reign of Darius king of the Persians.

text.KJV: 3:1 Now when Darius reigned, hee made a great feast vnto all his Subiects and vnto all his houshold, and vnto all the princes of Media and Persia,
text.NRSV: Now King Darius gave a great banquet for all that were under him and all that were born in his house and all the nobles of Media and Persia.
text.DRB: King Darius made a great supper to all his domestical servants, and to all the magistrates of Media and Persia,
and to all that wear purple, and to the Prætors, and Consuls, and Lieutenants under him from India unto Æthiopia, an hundred twenty seven provinces.
text.KJV: 3:2 And to all the gouernours and captaines, and lieutenants that were vnder him, from Iudia vnto Ethiopia, of an hundreth twenty and seuen prouinces.
text.NRSV: and all the satraps and generals and governors that were under him in the hundred and twenty-seven satrapies from India to Ethiopia.

text.DRB: And when they had eaten and drunken, and returned full, then Darius went up into his chamber, and slept, and awaked.
text.KJV: 3:3 And when they had eaten and drunken, and being satisfied were gone home, then Darius the king went into his bed chamber, and slept, and soone after awaked.
text.NRSV: They ate and drank, and when they were satisfied they went away, and King Darius went to his bedroom; he went to sleep, but woke up again.

text.KJV: 3:4 Then three yong men that were of the guard, that kept the kings body, spake one to another:
text.NRSV: Then the three young men of the bodyguard, who kept guard over the person of the king, said to one another,
text.DRB: Then those three young men keepers of his body, which guarded the king's body, said one to another:

text.KJV: 3:5 Let euery one of vs speake a sentence: hee that shall ouercome, & whose sentence shall seeme wiser then the others, vnto him shall the king Darius giue great gifts, and great things in token of victory:
text.NRSV: “Let each of us state what one thing is strongest; and to the one whose statement seems wisest, King Darius will give rich gifts and great honors of victory.”
text.DRB: Let every one of us say a word that may excel: & whose word soever shall appear wiser than the others, to him will king Darius give great gifts,
to be covered with purple, & to drink in gold, and to sleep upon gold, & a chariot with a bridle of gold, & a bonnet of silk, and a chain about his neck:
text.KJV: 3:6 As to be clothed in purple, to drink in golde, and to sleepe vpon golde, and a chariot with bridles of golde, and an head-tyre of fine linen, and a chaine about his necke:
text.NRSV: He shall be clothed in purple, and drink from gold cups, and sleep on a gold bed,] and have a chariot with gold bridles, and a turban of fine linen, and a necklace around his neck;

text.DRB: and he
text.KJV: 3:7 And hee shall sit next to Darius, because of his wisedome, and shalbe called, Darius his cousin.
text.NRSV: and because of his wisdom he shall sit next to Darius and shall be called Kinsman of Darius.’

text.DRB: Then every one writing his word signed it, and they put it under the pillow of Darius the king,
text.KJV: 3:8 And then euery one wrote his sentence, sealed it, and laide it vnder king Darius his pillow,
text.NRSV: Then each wrote his own statement, and they sealed them and put them under the pillow of King Darius,

text.DRB: and they said: When the king shall rise, we will give him our writings: and which soever of the three the king shall judge, and the magistrates of Persia, that his word is the wiser, to him shall the victory be given as is written.
text.KJV: 3:9 And sayd, that when the king is risen, some will giue him the writings, and of whose side the king, and the three princes of Persia shall iudge, that his sentence is the wisest, to him shall the victory be giuen as was appointed.
text.NRSV: and said, ‘When the king wakes, they will give him the writing; and to the one whose statement the king and the three nobles of Persia judge to be wisest the victory shall be given according to what is written.’

text.KJV: 3:10 The first wrote: Wine is the strongest.
text.NRSV: The first wrote, “Wine is strongest.”
text.DRB: One wrote: Wine is strong.

text.KJV: 3:11 The second wrote: The King is strongest.
text.NRSV: The second wrote, “The king is strongest.”
text.DRB: Another wrote, A king is stronger.

text.KJV: 3:12 The third wrote; Women are strongest, but aboue all things trueth beareth away the victory.
text.NRSV: The third wrote, “Women are strongest, but above all things truth is victor.”
text.DRB: The third wrote, Women are more strong: but above all things truth overcometh.
And when the king was risen, they took their writings, and gave him, and he read.
text.KJV: 3:13 ¶ Now when the king was risen vp, they tooke their writings, and deliuered them vnto him, and so hee read them.
text.NRSV: When the king awoke, they took the writing and gave it to him, and he read it.

text.DRB: And sending he called all the Magistrates of the Persians, and the Medes, and them that wear purple, and the Prætors, and the Overseers;
text.KJV: 3:14 And sending foorth, hee called all the Princes of Persia and Media, and the gouernours, and the captaines, and the lieutenants, and the chiefe officers,
text.NRSV: Then he sent and summoned all the nobles of Persia and Media and the satraps and generals and governors and prefects,

text.DRB: and they sat in the Councel: and the writings were read before them.
text.KJV: 3:15 And sate him downe in the royall seate of Iudgement, and the writings were read before them:
text.NRSV: and he took his seat in the council chamber, and the writing was read in their presence.

text.DRB: And he said: Call the young men, & they shall declare their own words. And they were called, and went in.
text.KJV: 3:16 And he said, Call the young men, and they shall declare their owne sentences: so they were called, and came in.
text.NRSV: He said, ‘Call the young men, and they shall explain their statements.’ So they were summoned, and came in.

text.DRB: And he said to them: Declare unto us concerning these things which are written. And the first began, he that had spoken of the strength of wine,
text.KJV: 3:17 And hee said vnto them, Declare vnto vs your minde, concerning the writings. Then began the first, who had spoken of the strength of wine;
text.NRSV: Then the first, who had spoken of the strength of wine, began and said:

text.DRB: and said: O ye men, how doth wine prevail over all men that drink! It seduceth the mind.
text.KJV: 3:18 And he said thus: O ye men, how exceeding strong is wine! it causeth all men to erre that drinke it:
text.NRSV: ‘Gentlemen, how is wine the strongest? It leads astray the minds of all who drink it.

text.DRB: And also the mind of king and orphan it maketh vain. Also of the bondman and the free, of the rich man and the poor,
text.KJV: 3:19 It maketh the minde of the king, and of the fatherlesse childe to be all one of the bondman and of the freeman, of the poore man and of the rich:
text.NRSV: It makes equal the mind of the king and the orphan, of the slave and the free, of the poor and the rich.

text.DRB: and every mind it turneth into security and pleasantness, and it remembereth not any sorrow and duty,
text.KJV: 3:20 It turneth also euery thought into iollitie and mirth, so that a man remembreth neither sorow nor debt:
text.NRSV: It turns every thought to feasting and mirth, and forgets all sorrow and debt.

text.DRB: and all hearts it maketh honest, and it remembereth not king, nor magistrate, and it maketh a man speak all things by talents.
text.KJV: 3:21 And it maketh euery heart rich, so that a man remembreth neither king nor gouernour, and it maketh to speake all things by talents:
text.NRSV: It makes all hearts feel rich, forgets kings and satraps, and makes everyone talk in millions.]

text.DRB: And when they have drunk, they remember not friendship, nor brotherhood: yea and not long after they take swords.
text.KJV: 3:22 And when they are in their cups, they forget their loue both to friends and brethren, and a litle after draw out swords:
text.NRSV: When people drink they forget to be friendly with friends and kindred, and before long they draw their swords.

text.DRB: And when they are recovered and risen from the wine, they remember not what they have done.
text.KJV: 3:23 But when they are from the wine, they remember not what they haue done.
text.NRSV: And when they recover from the wine, they do not remember what they have done.

text.DRB: O ye men, doth not wine excel? Who thinketh to do so? And having said this, he held his peace.
text.KJV: 3:24 O ye men, is not wine the strongest, that enforceth to doe thus? And when hee had so spoken, hee helde his peace.
text.NRSV: Gentlemen, is not wine the strongest, since it forces people to do these things?’ When he had said this, he stopped speaking.

text.DRB: And the next began to speak, he that spake of the strength of a king.
text.KJV: 4:1 Then the second that had spoken of the strength of the King, began to say;
text.NRSV: Then the second, who had spoken of the strength of the king, began to speak:

text.DRB: O ye men do not the men excel, which obtain land, & sea, and all things that are in them?
text.KJV: 4:2 O yee men, doe not men excel in strength, that beare rule ouer Sea and land, and all things in them?
text.NRSV: ‘Gentlemen, are not men strongest, who rule over land and sea and all that is in them?

text.DRB: But a king excelleth above all things, and hath dominion over them: and every thing whatsoever he shall say to them, they do.
text.KJV: 4:3 But yet the King is more mighty: for hee is lord of all these things, and hath dominion ouer them, and whatsoeuer he commandeth them, they doe:
text.NRSV: But the king is stronger; he is their lord and master, and whatever he says to them they obey.

text.DRB: And if he send them to warriors, they go, and throw down mountains, and the walls, and towers.
text.KJV: 4:4 If thee bid them make warre the one against the other, they doe it: if hee send them out against the enemies, they goe, and breake downe mountaines, walles and towres.
text.NRSV: If he tells them to make war on one another, they do it; and if he sends them out against the enemy, they go, and conquer mountains, walls, and towers.

text.DRB: They kill, and are killed: and the king's word they transgress not. For if they shall overcome, they bring to the king all things whatsoever they have taken for a prey.
text.KJV: 4:5 They slay and are slaine, and transgresse not the Kings commandement: if they get the victory, they bring all to the King, as well the spoile as all things else.
text.NRSV: They kill and are killed, and do not disobey the king’s command; if they win the victory, they bring everything to the king—whatever spoil they take and everything else.

text.DRB: In like manner also all others, for so many as are not soldiers, nor fight, but till the ground: when they shall reap, again they bring tributes to the king.
text.KJV: 4:6 Likewise for those that are no souldiers, and haue not to doe with warres, but vse husbandrie; when they haue reaped againe, that which they had sowen, they bring it to the King, and compell one another to pay tribute vnto the King.
text.NRSV: Likewise those who do not serve in the army or make war but till the soil; whenever they sow and reap, they bring some to the king; and they compel one another to pay taxes to the king.

text.DRB: And he being one only, if he say: Kill ye, they kill; say he: forgive, they forgive; say he: strike, they strike; say he, destroy, they destroy;
text.KJV: 4:7 And yet he is but one man; if hee commaund to kill, they kill, if he command to spare, they spare.
text.NRSV: And yet he is only one man! If he tells them to kill, they kill; if he tells them to release, they release;

text.DRB: say he build, they build;
text.KJV: 4:8 If he command to smite, they smite; if he command to make desolate, they make desolate; if hee command to build, they build:
text.NRSV: if he tells them to attack, they attack; if he tells them to lay waste, they lay waste; if he tells them to build, they build;

text.DRB: say he, cut down, they cut down; say he plant, they plant:
text.KJV: 4:9 If he command to cut downe, they cut downe; if he command to plant, they plant.
text.NRSV: if he tells them to cut down, they cut down; if he tells them to plant, they plant.

text.DRB: and all the people, & potestates hear him, and beside this he sitteth down, and drinketh, and sleepeth.
text.KJV: 4:10 So all his people and his armies obey him; furthermore he lieth downe, he eateth and drinketh, & taketh his rest.
text.NRSV: All his people and his armies obey him. Furthermore, he reclines, he eats and drinks and sleeps,

text.DRB: And others guard him round about, and cannot go every one, and do their own works, but at a word are obedient to him.
text.KJV: 4:11 And these keepe (watch) round about him, neither may any one depart, and doe his owne businesse, neither disobey they him in any thing.
text.NRSV: but they keep watch around him, and no one may go away to attend to his own affairs, nor do they disobey him.

text.DRB: O ye men, how doth not a king excel that is so renowned? And he held his peace.
text.KJV: 4:12 O yee men, how should not the King be mightiest, when in such sort he is obeyed? and he held his tongue.
text.NRSV: Gentlemen, why is not the king the strongest, since he is to be obeyed in this fashion?’ And he stopped speaking.

text.KJV: 4:13 ¶ Then the third, who had spoken of women, and of the truth (this was Zorobabel) beganne to speake.
text.NRSV: The third young man, who had spoken about women and truth (and this was Zerubbabel), began to speak:
text.DRB: The third that spake of women and truth, this is Zorobabel, began to speak.
O ye men, not the great king, & many men, neither is it wine that doth excel. Who is it then that hath the dominion of them?
text.KJV: 4:14 O yee men, it is not the great King, nor the multitude of men, neither is it wine that excelleth; who is it then that ruleth them, or hath the lordship ouer them, are they not women?
text.NRSV: ‘Gentlemen, is not the king great, and are not men many, and is not wine strong? Who is it, then, that rules them, or has the mastery over them? Is it not women?

text.DRB: Have not women brought forth the king, & all the people, that ruleth over land & sea:
text.KJV: 4:15 Women haue borne the King and all the people, that beare rule by sea and land.
text.NRSV: Women gave birth to the king and to every people that rules over sea and land.

text.DRB: and were they not born of them, and did not they bring up them which planted the vineyards, whereof wine is made?
text.KJV: 4:16 Euen of thē came they: & they nourished them vp that planted the vineyards from whence the wine commeth.
text.NRSV: From women they came; and women brought up the very men who plant the vineyards from which comes wine.

text.DRB: And they make the garments of all men, & they do honour to all men, and men cannot be separated from women.
text.KJV: 4:17 These also make garments for men; these bring glory vnto men, and without women cannot men be.
text.NRSV: Women make men’s clothes; they bring men glory; men cannot exist without women.

text.DRB: If they have gathered gold and silver, and
text.KJV: 4:18 Yea and if men haue gathered together gold and siluer, or any other goodly thing, doe they not loue a woman, which is comely in fauour and beautie?
text.NRSV: If men gather gold and silver or any other beautiful thing, and then see a woman lovely in appearance and beauty,

text.DRB: leaving all these things they fix their look upon her, & with open mouth behold her, and allure her more than gold & silver, and every precious thing.
text.KJV: 4:19 And letting all those things goe, doe they not gape, and euen with open mouth fixe their eyes fast on her; and haue not all men more desire vnto her, then vnto siluer or gold, or any goodly thing whatsoeuer?
text.NRSV: they let all those things go, and gape at her, and with open mouths stare at her, and all prefer her to gold or silver or any other beautiful thing.

text.DRB: Man forsaketh his father that brought him up, and his country, and joineth himself to a woman.
text.KJV: 4:20 A man leaueth his owne father that brought him vp, and his owne countrie, and cleaueth vnto his wife.
text.NRSV: A man leaves his own father, who brought him up, and his own country, and clings to his wife.

text.DRB: And with a woman he refresheth his soul: and neither doth he remember father, nor mother, nor country.
text.KJV: 4:21 He stickes not to spend his life with his wife, and remembreth neither father, nor mother, nor countrey.
text.NRSV: With his wife he ends his days, with no thought of his father or his mother or his country.

text.DRB: And hereby you must know that women rule over you. Are you not sorry?
text.KJV: 4:22 By this also you must know, that women haue dominion ouer you: doe yee not labour and toyle, and giue and bring all to the woman?
text.NRSV: ‘Do you not labour and toil, and bring everything and give it to women?

text.DRB: And a man taketh his sword, & goeth into the way to commit thefts and murders, & to sail seas & rivers,
text.KJV: 4:23 Yea a man taketh his sword, and goeth his way to rob, and to steale, to saile vpon the sea, and vpon riuers,
text.NRSV: A man takes his sword, and goes out to travel and rob and steal and to sail the sea and rivers;

text.DRB: and seeth a lion; and goeth in darkness: and when he hath committed theft, and fraud, and spoils, he bringeth it to his beloved.
text.KJV: 4:24 And looketh vpon a lyon, and goeth in the darknesse, and when he hath stolen, spoiled and robbed, he bringeth it to his loue.
text.NRSV: he faces lions, and he walks in darkness, and when he steals and robs and plunders, he brings it back to the woman he loves.

text.DRB: And again, man loveth his wife more than father or mother.
text.KJV: 4:25 Wherefore a man loueth his wife better then father and mother.
text.NRSV: A man loves his wife more than his father or his mother.

text.DRB: And many have become mad for their wives: & have been made bondmen for them:
text.KJV: 4:26 Yea many there be that haue run out of their wits for women, and become seruants for their sakes:
text.NRSV: Many men have lost their minds because of women, and have become slaves because of them.

text.DRB: and many have perished and been slain, and have sinned for women.
text.KJV: 4:27 Many also haue perished, haue erred, and sinned for women.
text.NRSV: Many have perished, or stumbled, or sinned because of women.

text.DRB: And now believe me, that the king is great in his power: because all countries are afraid to touch him.
text.KJV: 4:28 And now doe yee not belieue me? is not the King great in his power? doe not all regions feare to touch him?
text.NRSV: ‘Is not the king great in his power? Do not all lands fear to touch him?

text.DRB: Nevertheless I saw Apemes the daughter of Bezaces the concubine of a marvelous king, sitting by the king at his right hand,
text.KJV: 4:29 Yet did I see him and Apame the Kings concubine, the daughter of the admirable Bartacus, sitting at the right hand of the King,
text.NRSV: Yet I have seen him with Apame, the king’s concubine, the daughter of the illustrious Bartacus; she would sit at the king’s right hand

text.DRB: and taking off the crown from his head, and putting it upon herself, and with the palm of her left hand she struck the king.
text.KJV: 4:30 And taking the crowne from the Kings head, and setting it vpon her owne head; she also strooke the King with her left hand.
text.NRSV: and take the crown from the king’s head and put it on her own, and slap the king with her left hand.

text.DRB: And beside these things he with open mouth beheld her: and if she smile he laugheth, and if she be angry with him, he flattereth, till he be reconciled to her favour.
text.KJV: 4:31 And yet for all this, the King gaped and gazed vpon her with open mouth: if she laughed vpon him, hee laughed also: but if she tooke any displeasure at him, the King was faine to flatter, that she might be reconciled to him againe.
text.NRSV: At this the king would gaze at her with mouth agape. If she smiles at him, he laughs; if she loses her temper with him, he flatters her, so that she may be reconciled to him.

text.DRB: O ye men, why are not women stronger? Great is the earth, and high is the heaven: who doeth these things?
text.KJV: 4:32 O ye men, how can it be but women should be strong, seeing they doe thus?
text.NRSV: Gentlemen, why are not women strong, since they do such things?’

text.KJV: 4:33 Then the king & the princes looked one vpon another: so he began to speake of the trueth.
text.NRSV: “Gentlemen, is not the king great, and are not men many, and is not wine strong? Who is it, then, that rules them, or has the mastery over them? Is it not women?”
text.DRB: And then the king and they that wear purple looked one upon another. And he began to speak of truth.
O ye men, are not women strong? The earth is great and heaven is high: & the swift course of the sun turneth the heaven round into his place in one day.
text.KJV: 4:34 O ye men, are not women strong? great is the earth, high is the heauen, swift is the Sunne in his course, for he compasseth the heauens round about, and fetcheth his course againe to his owne place in one day.
text.NRSV: ‘Gentlemen, are not women strong? The earth is vast, and heaven is high, and the sun is swift in its course, for it makes the circuit of the heavens and returns to its place in one day.

text.DRB: Is not he magnifical that doth these things, and the truth great, and stronger above all things?
text.KJV: 4:35 Is he not great that maketh these things? therefore great is the truth, and stronger then all things.
text.NRSV: Is not the one who does these things great? But truth is great, and stronger than all things.

text.DRB: All the earth calleth upon the truth, heaven also blesseth it, and all works are moved, and tremble at it, and there is not any thing with it unjust.
text.KJV: 4:36 All the earth calleth vpon the truth, & the heauen blesseth it, all works shake and tremble at it, and with it is no vnrighteous thing.
text.NRSV: The whole earth calls upon truth, and heaven blesses it. All God’s works] quake and tremble, and with him there is nothing unrighteous.

text.DRB: Wine is unjust, the king is unjust, women are unjust, all the sons of men are unjust, and all their works are unjust, and in them is not truth, and they shall perish in their iniquity,
text.KJV: 4:37 Wine is wicked, the king is wicked, women are wicked, all the children of men are wicked, and such are all their wicked workes, and there is no trueth in them. In their vnrighteousnes also they shall perish.
text.NRSV: Wine is unrighteous, the king is unrighteous, women are unrighteous, all human beings are unrighteous, all their works are unrighteous, and all such things. There is no truth in them and in their unrighteousness they will perish.

text.KJV: 4:38 As for the trueth it endureth, and is alwayes strong, it liueth and conquereth for euermore.
text.NRSV: “But truth is great, and stronger than all things.”
text.DRB: and truth abideth, and groweth strong for ever, and liveth, and prevaileth for ever and ever.
Neither is there with it acception of persons, nor differences: but the things that are just it doth to all men, to the unjust and malignant, and all men are well pleased in the works thereof.
text.KJV: 4:39 With her there is no accepting of persons, or rewards, but she doeth the things that are iust, and refraineth from all vniust and wicked things, and all men doe well like of her workes.
text.NRSV: With it there is no partiality or preference, but it does what is righteous instead of anything that is unrighteous or wicked. Everyone approves its deeds,

text.DRB: And there is no unjust thing in the judgement thereof, but strength, and reign, and power, and majesty of worlds. Blessed be the God of truth.
text.KJV: 4:40 Neither in her iudgement is any vnrighteousnesse, & she is the strength, kingdome, power and maiestie of all ages. Blessed be the God of trueth.
text.NRSV: and there is nothing unrighteous in its judgement. To it belongs the strength and the kingship and the power and the majesty of all the ages. Blessed be the God of truth!’

text.KJV: 4:41 And with that he held his peace, and al the people then shouted and said, Great is trueth, and mightie aboue all things.
text.NRSV: When he stopped speaking, all the people then shouted and said, “Great is truth, and strongest of all!”
text.DRB: And he left speaking. And all the people cried, and said: Great is truth and it prevaileth.

text.KJV: 4:42 Then saide the king vnto him, Aske what thou wilt, more then is appointed in the writing, and we wil giue it thee, because thou art found wisest, and thou shalt sit next me, and shalt bee called my cousin.
text.NRSV: Then the king said to him, “Ask what you wish, even beyond what is written, and we will give it to you.”
text.DRB: Then the king said to him: Ask, if thou wilt any more, than the things that are written, and I will give it thee, according as thou art found wiser than thy neighbours, and thou shalt sit next to me, and shalt be called my cousin.

text.KJV: 4:43 Then said hee vnto the king, Remember thy vow which thou hast vowed to build Ierusalem in the day when thou camest to the kingdome,
text.NRSV: Then he said to the king, “Remember the vow that you made on the day when you took your kingdom, to build Jerusalem.”
text.DRB: Then said he to the king: Be mindful of thy vow, which thou hast vowed, to build Jerusalem in the day that thou didst receive the kingdom:
and to send back all the vessels that were taken out of Jerusalem, which Cyrus separated, when he sacked Babylon, and would have sent them back thither.
text.KJV: 4:44 And to send away all the vessels that were taken away out of Ierusalem, which Cyrus set apart, when hee vowed to destroy Babylon, and to send them againe thither.
text.NRSV: and to send back all the vessels that were taken from Jerusalem, which Cyrus set apart when he began] to destroy Babylon, and vowed to send them back there.

text.DRB: And thou hast vowed to build the temple, which the Idumeians burnt, when Jury was destroyed of the Chaldees.
text.KJV: 4:45 Thou also hast vowed to build vp the Temple, which the Edomites burnt when Iudea was made desolate by the Chaldees.
text.NRSV: You also vowed to build the temple, which the Edomites burned when Judea was laid waste by the Chaldeans.

text.DRB: And now this is that which I ask lord, & which I desire, this is the majesty which I desire of thee, that thou perform the vow which thou hast vowed to the king of heaven by thy mouth.
text.KJV: 4:46 And now, O lord the king, this is that which I require, and which I desire of thee, and this is the princely liberalitie proceeding from thy selfe: I desire therefore that thou make good the vow, the performance wherof with thine owne mouth thou hast vowed to the king of heauen.
text.NRSV: And now, O lord the king, this is what I ask and request of you, and this befits your greatness. I pray therefore that you fulfil the vow whose fulfilment you vowed to the King of heaven with your own lips.’

text.DRB: Then Darius the king rising up, kissed him: and wrote letters to all the officers, and overseers, and them that wear purple, that they should conduct him, and them that were with him, all going up to build Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 4:47 Then Darius the king stood vp and kissed him, and wrote letters for him vnto all the treasurers and lieutenants, and captaines and gouernours that they should safely conuey on their way, both him, and all those that go vp with him to build Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: Then King Darius got up and kissed him, and wrote letters for him to all the treasurers and governors and generals and satraps, that they should give safe conduct to him and to all who were going up with him to build Jerusalem.

text.DRB: And to all the overseers that were in Syria, and Phoenice, and Libanus he wrote letters, that they should draw Cedar trees from Libanus into Jerusalem, to build the city with them.
text.KJV: 4:48 Hee wrote letters also vnto the lieutenants that were in Coelosyria and Phenice, and vnto them in Libanus, that they should bring Cedar wood from Libanus vnto Ierusalem, and that they should build the city with him
text.NRSV: And he wrote letters to all the governors in Coelesyria and Phoenicia and to those in Lebanon, to bring cedar timber from Lebanon to Jerusalem, and to help him build the city.

text.DRB: And he wrote to all the Jews which went up from the Kingdom into Jury for liberty, every mighty man, & magistrate, & overseer not to come upon them to their gates,
text.KJV: 4:49 Moreouer he wrote for all the Iewes that went out of his realme vp into Iurie, concerning their freedome, that no officer, no ruler, no lieutenant, nor treasurer, should forcibly enter into their dores,
text.NRSV: He wrote in behalf of all the Jews who were going up from his kingdom to Judea, in the interest of their freedom, that no officer or satrap or governor or treasurer should forcibly enter their doors;

text.DRB: and all the country which they had obtained to be free unto them, & that the Idumeians leave the castles which they possess of the Jews,
text.KJV: 4:50 And that all the countrey which they hold, should be free without tribute, & that the Edomites should giue ouer the villages of the Iewes which then they held,
text.NRSV: that all the country that they would occupy should be theirs without tribute; that the Idumeans should give up the villages of the Jews that they held;

text.DRB: and to the building of the temple to give every year twenty talents until it were throughly built:
text.KJV: 4:51 Yea that there should be yereely giuen twentie talents to þ building of the Temple, vntill ye time that it were built,
text.NRSV: that twenty talents a year should be given for the building of the temple until it was completed,

text.DRB: & upon the altars to burn holocausts daily, as they have commandment: to offer
text.KJV: 4:52 And other tenne talents yeerely, to maintaine the burnt offerings vpon the Altar euery day (as they had a commandement to offer seuenteene)
text.NRSV: and an additional ten talents a year for burnt-offerings to be offered on the altar every day, in accordance with the commandment to make seventeen offerings;

text.DRB: & to all that go forth from Babylon to build the city, that there should be liberty as well to them as to their children, and to all the Priests that go before.
text.KJV: 4:53 And that all they that went from Babylon to build the citie, should haue free liberty as well they as their posteritie, and all the priests that went away.
text.NRSV: and that all who came from Babylonia to build the city should have their freedom, they and their children and all the priests who came.

text.DRB: And he wrote a quantity also, and commanded the sacred stole to be given, wherein they should serve;
text.KJV: 4:54 He wrote also concerning the charges, and the priests vestments wherein they minister:
text.NRSV: He wrote also concerning their support and the priests’ vestments in which] they were to minister.

text.DRB: and to the Levites he wrote to give precepts, until the day wherein the house shall be finished, and Jerusalem builded.
text.KJV: 4:55 And likewise for the charges of the Leuites, to be giuen them, vntill the day that the house were finished, and Ierusalem builded vp.
text.NRSV: He wrote that the support for the Levites should be provided until the day when the temple would be finished and Jerusalem built.

text.DRB: And to all that keep the city, he wrote portions and wages to be given to them.
text.KJV: 4:56 And he commanded to giue to all that kept the city, pensions and wages.
text.NRSV: He wrote that land and wages should be provided for all who guarded the city.

text.DRB: And he sent away all the vessels whatsoever Cyrus had separated from Babylon, and all things whatsoever Cyrus said, he also commanded to be done, & to be sent to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 4:57 He sent away also all the vessels frō Babylon that Cyrus had set apart, and all that Cyrus had giuen in commandement, the same charged hee also to be done, and sent vnto Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: And he sent back from Babylon all the vessels that Cyrus had set apart; everything that Cyrus had ordered to be done, he also commanded to be done and to be sent to Jerusalem.

text.DRB: And when that young man was gone forth, lifting up his face toward Jerusalem, he blessed the King of heaven,
text.KJV: 4:58 Now when this yong man was gone forth, he lifted vp his face to heauen toward Ierusalem, and praised the king of heauen,
text.NRSV: When the young man went out, he lifted up his face to heaven towards Jerusalem, and praised the King of heaven, saying,

text.DRB: and said: Of thee is victory, and of thee is wisdom, and glory. And I am thy servant.
text.KJV: 4:59 And said, From thee commeth victory, from thee commeth wisedom, and thine is the glory, & I am thy seruant.
text.NRSV: ‘From you comes the victory; from you comes wisdom, and yours is the glory. I am your servant.

text.DRB: Blessed art thou which hast given me wisdom, and I will confess to thee Lord God of our fathers.
text.KJV: 4:60 Blessed art thou who hast giuen me wisedom: for to thee I giue thanks, O Lord of our fathers.
text.NRSV: Blessed are you, who have given me wisdom; I give you thanks, O Lord of our ancestors.’

text.DRB: And he took the letters, and went into Babylon. And he came, and told all his brethren that were in Babylon:
text.KJV: 4:61 And so he tooke the letters, and went out, and came vnto Babylon, and told it all his brethren.
text.NRSV: So he took the letters, and went to Babylon and told this to all his kindred.

text.DRB: and they blessed the God of their fathers, because he gave them remission and refreshing,
text.KJV: 4:62 And they praised the God of their fathers: because he had giuen them freedome and libertie
text.NRSV: And they praised the God of their ancestors, because he had given them release and permission

text.DRB: that they should go up and build Jerusalem, and the temple wherein his name was renowned, and they rejoiced with music & joy seven days.
text.KJV: 4:63 To goe vp, and to build Ierusalem, and the Temple which is called by his Name, and they feasted with instruments of musick, & gladnes seuen dayes.
text.NRSV: to go up and build Jerusalem and the temple that is called by his name; and they feasted, with music and rejoicing, for seven days.

text.KJV: 5:1 After this were the principall men of the families chosen according to their tribes, to go vp with their wiues, and sonnes, and daughters, with their men-seruants and maid-seruants, and their cattel.
text.NRSV: After this the heads of ancestral houses were chosen to go up, according to their tribes, with their wives and sons and daughters, with their male and female servants, and their livestock.
text.DRB: After these things were chosen to go up the Princes of towns by their houses, and tribes, and their wives, and their sons and daughters, & their men servants and women servants, and their cattle.
And Darius the king sent together with them a thousand horsemen, till they conducted them to Jerusalem with peace, & with music, & with timbrels, and shalms:
text.KJV: 5:2 And Darius sent with them a thousand horsmen, til they had brought them backe to Ierusalem safely, and with musicall  tabrets and flutes:
text.NRSV: And Darius sent with them a thousand cavalry to take them back to Jerusalem in safety, with the music of drums and flutes;

text.DRB: and all the brethren were playing, and he made them go up together with them.
text.KJV: 5:3 And all their brethren played, and hee made them goe vp together with them.
text.NRSV: all their kindred were making merry. And he made them go up with them.

text.DRB: And these are the names of the men that went up by their towns according to tribes, & according to the portion of their principality.
text.KJV: 5:4 And these are the names of the men which went vp, according to their families, amongst their tribes, after their seuerall heads.
text.NRSV: These are the names of the men who went up, according to their ancestral houses in the tribes, over their groups:

text.DRB: Priests: The children of Phinees, the son of Aaron, Iesus the son of Iosedec, Ioacim the son of Zorobabel, the son of Salathiel of the house of David, of the progeny of Phares, of the tribe of Juda.
text.KJV: 5:5 The Priestes the sonnes of Phinees, the sonne of Aaron: Iesus the sonne of Iosedec, the sonne of Saraias, and Ioachim the sonne of Zorobabel, the sonne of Salathiel of the house of Dauid, out of the kindred of Phares, of the tribe of Iuda;
text.NRSV: the priests, the descendants of Phinehas son of Aaron; Jeshua son of Jozadak son of Seraiah and Joakim son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, of the house of David, of the lineage of Phares, of the tribe of Judah,

text.DRB: Who spake under Darius king of the Persians the marvelous words in the second year of his reign the first month Nisan.
text.KJV: 5:6 Who spake wise sentences before Darius the king of Persia, in the second yeere of his reigne, in the moneth Nisan, which is the first moneth.
text.NRSV: who spoke wise words before King Darius of the Persians, in the second year of his reign, in the month of Nisan, the first month.

text.DRB: And they are these, that of Jury came up from the captivity of the transmigration, whom Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon transported into Babylon, and returned into Jerusalem. 1.
text.KJV: 5:7 And these are they of Iewrie that came vp from the captiuitie, where they dwelt as strangers, whom Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon had carried away vnto Babylon:
text.NRSV: These are the Judeans who came up out of their sojourn in exile, whom King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon had carried away to Babylon

text.DRB: And every one sought a part of Jury according to his own city, they that came with Zorobabel, and Iesus, Nehemias, Areores, Elimeo, Emmanio, Mardocheo, Beelsuro, Mechpsatochor, Olioro, Emonia one of their Princes.
text.KJV: 5:8 And they returned vnto Ierusalem, and to the other parts of Iurie euery man to his owne city, who came with Zorobabel, with Iesus, Nehemias, and Zacharias, and Reesaias, Enenius, Mardocheus, Beelsarus, Aspharasus, Reelius, Roimus, and Baana their guides.
text.NRSV: and who returned to Jerusalem and the rest of Judea, each to his own town. They came with Zerubbabel and Jeshua, Nehemiah, Seraiah, Resaiah, Eneneus, Mordecai, Beelsarus, Aspharasus, Reeliah, Rehum, and Baanah, their leaders.
