text.DRB: And the number of them of the same nation, of their rulers the children of Phares, two thousand an hundred seventy two:
text.KJV: 5:9 The number of them of the nation, and their gouernours: sonnes of Phoros two thousand an hundred seuentie and two: the sonnes of Saphat foure hundred seuentie and two;
text.NRSV: The number of those of the nation and their leaders: the descendants of Parosh, two thousand one hundred and seventy-two. The descendants of Shephatiah, four hundred and seventy-two.

text.DRB: The children of Ares, three thousand an hundred fifty seven:
text.KJV: 5:10 The sonnes of Ares seuen hundred fiftie and sixe:
text.NRSV: The descendants of Arah, seven hundred and fifty-six.

text.DRB: The children of Phoemo, and hundred forty two: in the children of Iesus & Ioabes, a thousand three hundred two:
text.KJV: 5:11 The sonnes of Phaath Moab, two thousand eight hundred & twelue:
text.NRSV: The descendants of Pahath-moab, of the descendants of Jeshua and Joab, two thousand eight hundred and twelve.

text.DRB: the children of Demu, two thousand four hundred seventy: the children of Choraba, two hundred five: the children of Banica, an hundred sixty eight,
text.KJV: 5:12 The sonnes of Elam, a thousand two hundred fifty and foure: the sonnes of Zathui, nine hundred fourtie and fiue: the sonnes of Corbe seuen hundred and fiue: the sonnes of Bani, sixe hundred fourtie and eight:
text.NRSV: The descendants of Elam, one thousand two hundred and fifty-four. The descendants of Zattu, nine hundred and forty-five. The descendants of Chorbe, seven hundred and five. The descendants of Bani, six hundred and forty-eight.

text.DRB: the children of Bebech, four hundred three: the children of Archad, four hundred twenty seven:
text.KJV: 5:13 The sonnes of Bebai, sixe hundred twentie and three: the sonnes of Sadas, three thousand two hundred twentie and two:
text.NRSV: The descendants of Bebai, six hundred and twenty-three. The descendants of Azgad, one thousand three hundred and twenty-two.

text.DRB: the children of Cham, thirty seven: the children of Zoroar, two thousand sixty seven: the children of Adin, four hundred sixty one:
text.KJV: 5:14 The sonnes of Adonican, sixe hundred sixtie and seuen: the sonnes of Bagoi, two thousand sixtie and sixe: the sonnes of Adin, foure hundred fiftie and foure:
text.NRSV: The descendants of Adonikam, six hundred and sixty-seven. The descendants of Bigvai, two thousand and sixty-six. The descendants of Adin, four hundred and fifty-four.

text.DRB: the children of Aderectes, an hundred eight: the children of Ciaso and Zelas an hundred seven: the children of Azoroc, four hundred thirty nine:
text.KJV: 5:15 The sonnes of Aterezias, ninetie and two: the sonnes of Ceilan and Azetas, threescore and seuen: the sonnes of Azuran, foure hundred thirtie & two.
text.NRSV: The descendants of Ater, namely of Hezekiah, ninety-two. The descendants of Kilan and Azetas, sixty-seven. The descendants of Azaru, four hundred and thirty-two.

text.DRB: the children of Iedarbone, an hundred, thirty two: the children of Ananias, an hundred thirty: the children of Asoni, ninety:
text.KJV: 5:16 The sonnes of Ananias, an hundred and one: the sonnes of Arom thirtie two, and the sonnes of Bassa, three hundred twentie and three: the sonnes of Azephurith, an hundred and two:
text.NRSV: The descendants of Annias, one hundred and one. The descendants of Arom. The descendants of Bezai, three hundred and twenty-three. The descendants of Arsiphurith, one hundred and twelve.

text.DRB: the children of Marsar, four hundred twenty two: the children of Zabarus, ninety five: the children of
text.KJV: 5:17 The sonnes of Meterus, three thousand and fiue: the sonnes of Bethlomon, an hundred twentie and three.
text.NRSV: The descendants of Baiterus, three thousand and five. The descendants of Bethlomon, one hundred and twenty-three.

text.DRB: the children of Nepopas, fifty five: the children of Hechanatus, an hundred fifty eight: the children of Cebethamus, an hundred thirty two:
text.KJV: 5:18 They of Netophah fiftie and fiue: they of Anathoth, an hundred fiftie and eight: they of Bethsamos, fourtie and two:
text.NRSV: Those from Netophah, fifty-five. Those from Anathoth, one hundred and fifty-eight. Those from Bethasmoth, forty-two.

text.NRSV: Now in the second year of the reign of Darius, the prophets Haggai and Zechariah son of Iddo prophesied to the Jews who were in Judea and Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And in the second year of the reign of Darius prophesied Aggeus, & Zacharias the son of Addo the Prophet to Jury & Jerusalem in the name of God of Israel upon them.

text.KJV: 6:2 Then stood vp Zorobabel the sonne of Salathiel, and Iesus the son of Iosedec, and beganne to build the house of the Lord at Ierusalem, the prophets of the Lord being with them,and helping them.
text.NRSV: Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Jeshua son of Jozadak began to build the house of the Lord that is in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Then Zorobabel the son of Salathiel standing up, and Iesus the son of Iosedec, began to build the house of our Lord, which is in Jerusalem.
When the Prophets of our Lord were present with them, and did help them. At the same time came Sisennes to them, the deputy of Syria, and of Phenice, and Satrabuzanes, and his fellows:
text.KJV: 6:3 At the same time came vnto them Sisinnes the gouernor of Syria, and Phenice, with Sathrabuzanes, and his companions, and said vnto them,
text.NRSV: At the same time Sisinnes the governor of Syria and Phoenicia and Sathrabuzanes and their associates came to them and said,

text.DRB: and they said to them: By whose commandment build ye this house, and this roof, and perfit all other things? And who are the workmen that build these things?
text.KJV: 6:4 By whose appointment doe you build this house, and this roofe, and performe all the other things? and who are the workemen that performe these things?
text.NRSV: ‘By whose order are you building this house and this roof and finishing all the other things? And who are the builders that are finishing these things?’

text.DRB: And the ancients of the Jews, which were left of the captivity by our Lord, had favour when the visitation was made upon them.
text.KJV: 6:5 Neuerthelesse the Elders of the Iewes obtained fauour: because the Lord had visited the captiuitie.
text.NRSV: Yet the elders of the Jews were dealt with kindly, for the providence of the Lord was over the captives;

text.DRB: And they were not hindered from building, till it was signified to Darius of all these things, and answer was received.
text.KJV: 6:6 And they were not hindred from building vntil such time as signification was giuen vnto Darius concerning them, and an answere receiued.
text.NRSV: they were not prevented from building until word could be sent to Darius concerning them and a report made.

text.DRB: A copy of the letter, which they sent to Darius. SISENNES deputy of Syria & Phenice, and Satrabuzanes, and his fellows in Syria and Phenice Presidents, to king Darius greeting.
text.KJV: 6:7 The copie of the letters which Sisinnes gouernour of Syria, and Phenice, and Sathrabuzanes with their companions rulers in Syria and Phenice, wrote and sent vnto Darius, To king Darius, greeting.
text.NRSV: A copy of the letter that Sisinnes the governor of Syria and Phoenicia, and Sathrabuzanes, and their associates the local rulers in Syria and Phoenicia, wrote and sent to Darius:

text.DRB: Be all things known to our lord the king, that when we came into the country of Jury, and had entered into Jerusalem, we found them building the great house of God.
text.KJV: 6:8 Let all things bee knowen vnto our lord the King, that being come into the countrey of Iudea, and entred into the citie of Ierusalem, we found in the citie of Ierusalem the ancients of the Iewes that were of the captiuitie;
text.NRSV: ‘To King Darius, greetings. Let it be fully known to our lord the king that, when we went to the country of Judea and entered the city of Jerusalem, we found the elders of the Jews, who had been in exile,

text.DRB: And the temple of polished stones, and of great & precious matter in the walls.
text.KJV: 6:9 Building an house vnto the Lord, great, and newe, of hewen and costly stones, and the timber already laid vpon the walles.
text.NRSV: building in the city of Jerusalem a great new house for the Lord, of hewn stone, with costly timber laid in the walls.

text.DRB: And the works to be a doing earnestly, and to succeed, and prosper in their hands, and in all glory to be perfited most diligently.
text.KJV: 6:10 And those workes are done with great speede, and the worke goeth on prosperously in their handes, and with all glory and diligence is it made.
text.NRSV: These operations are going on rapidly, and the work is prospering in their hands and being completed with all splendour and care.

text.DRB: Then we asked the ancients saying, by whose permission build ye this house, and found these works?
text.KJV: 6:11 Then asked wee these Elders, saying, By whose commaundement builde you this house, and lay the foundations of these workes?
text.NRSV: Then we asked these elders, “At whose command are you building this house and laying the foundations of this structure?”

text.DRB: And therefore we asked them, that we might do thee to know the men & the overseers, and we required of them a roll of the names of the overseers.
text.KJV: 6:12 Therefore to the intent that wee might giue knowledge vnto thee by writing, we demanded of them who were the chiefe doers, and we required of them the names in writing of their principall men.
text.NRSV: In order that we might inform you in writing who the leaders are, we questioned them and asked them for a list of the names of those who are at their head.

text.DRB: But they answered us saying: We are the servants of the Lord, which made heaven and earth.
text.KJV: 6:13 So they gaue vs this answere: We are the seruants of the Lord which made heauen and earth.
text.NRSV: They answered us, “We are the servants of the Lord who created the heaven and the earth.

text.DRB: And this house was built these many years past by a king of Israel, that was great and most valiant, & was finished.
text.KJV: 6:14 And as for this house, it was builded many yeeres agoe, by a king of Israel great and strong, and was finished.
text.NRSV: The house was built many years ago by a king of Israel who was great and strong, and it was finished.

text.DRB: And because our fathers were provoking to wrath, and sinned against God of Israel, he delivered them into the hands of Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon, king of the Chaldees.
text.KJV: 6:15 But when our fathers prouoked God vnto wrath, and sinned against the Lord of Israel which is in heauen, hee gaue them ouer into the power of Nabuchodonosor king of Babylon of the Chaldees:
text.NRSV: But when our ancestors sinned against the Lord of Israel who is in heaven, and provoked him, he gave them over into the hands of King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon, king of the Chaldeans;

text.DRB: And throwing down this house they burnt it, and they led the people captive into Babylon.
text.KJV: 6:16 Who pulled downe the house and burnt it, and caried away the people captiues vnto Babylon.
text.NRSV: and they pulled down the house, and burned it, and carried the people away captive to Babylon.

text.DRB: In the first year when Cyrus reigned the king of Babylon, Cyrus the king wrote to build this house.
text.KJV: 6:17 But in the first yeere that King Cyrus reigned ouer the country of Babylon, Cyrus the king wrote to build vp this house.
text.NRSV: But in the first year that Cyrus reigned over the country of Babylonia, King Cyrus wrote that this house should be rebuilt.

text.DRB: And these sacred vessels of gold and silver which Nabuchodonosor had taken out of the house which is in Jerusalem, and had consecrated them in his own temple, Cyrus brought them forth again out of the temple which was in Babylon, and they were delivered to Zorobabel, & to Salmanasar, the deputy.
text.KJV: 6:18 And the holy vessels of gold and of siluer, that Nabuchodonosor had caried away out of the house at Ierusalem, and had set them in his owne temple, those Cyrus the king brought forth againe out of the temple at Babylon, and they were deliuered to Zorobabel and to Sanabassarus the ruler,
text.NRSV: And the holy vessels of gold and of silver, which Nebuchadnezzar had taken out of the house in Jerusalem and stored in his own temple, these King Cyrus took out again from the temple in Babylon, and they were delivered to Zerubbabel and Sheshbazzar] the governor

text.DRB: And it was commanded them that they should offer these vessels, & lay them up in the temple, which was in Jerusalem, & build the temple of God itself in his place.
text.KJV: 6:19 With commaundement that hee should carrie away the same vessels, and put them in the Temple at Ierusalem, and that the Temple of þ Lord should be built in his place.
text.NRSV: with the command that he should take all these vessels back and put them in the temple at Jerusalem, and that this temple of the Lord should be rebuilt on its site.

text.DRB: Then did Salmanasar lay the foundations of the house of our Lord, which is in Jerusalem: & from that time until now it is a building, & is not accomplished.
text.KJV: 6:20 Then the same Sanabassarus being come hither, laid the foundations of the house of the Lord at Ierusalem, and from that time to this, being still a building, it is not yet fully ended.
text.NRSV: Then this Sheshbazzar, after coming here, laid the foundations of the house of the Lord that is in Jerusalem. Although it has been in process of construction from that time until now, it has not yet reached completion.”

text.DRB: Now therefore if thou think it good o king, let it be sought in the kings' libraries of Cyrus the king, which are in Babylon.
text.KJV: 6:21 Now therefore if it seeme good vnto the king, let search be made among the records of King Cyrus,
text.NRSV: Now therefore, O king, if it seems wise to do so, let search be made in the royal archives of our lord] the king that are in Babylon;

text.DRB: and if it shall be found, that the building of the house of the Lord, which is in Jerusalem, began by the counsel of Cyrus the king, and it be thought good of our Lord the king, let him write to us of these things.
text.KJV: 6:22 And if it be found, that the building of the house of the Lord at Ierusalem hath bene done with the consent of King Cyrus, and if our lord the king be so minded, let him signifie vnto vs thereof.
text.NRSV: if it is found that the building of the house of the Lord in Jerusalem was done with the consent of King Cyrus, and if it is approved by our lord the king, let him send us directions concerning these things.’

text.DRB: Then Darius the king commanded search to be made in the libraries: and there was found in Ecbatana a town that is in the country of Media, one place wherein were written these words.
text.KJV: 6:23 Then commanded king Darius to seeke among the records at Babylon: and so at Ecbatana the palace which is in the countrey of Media, there was found a roule wherein these things were recorded.
text.NRSV: Then Darius commanded that search be made in the royal archives that were deposited in Babylon. And in Ecbatana, the fortress that is in the country of Media, a scroll] was found in which this was recorded:

text.DRB: IN THE FIRST YEAR of the reign of Cyrus, king Cyrus commanded to build the house of the Lord which is in Jerusalem, where they did burn incense with daily fire,
text.KJV: 6:24 In the first yeere of the reigne of Cyrus, king Cyrus commaunded that the house of the Lord at Ierusalem should bee built againe where they doe sacrifice with continuall fire.
text.NRSV: ‘In the first year of the reign of King Cyrus, he ordered the building of the house of the Lord in Jerusalem, where they sacrifice with perpetual fire;

text.DRB: the height whereof shall be of ten cubits, & the breadth three score cubits, four square
text.KJV: 6:25 Whose height shalbe sixtie cubits, and the breadth sixtie cubits, with three rowes of hewen stones, and one row of new wood of that countrey, and the expenses thereof to bee giuen out of the house of king Cyrus.
text.NRSV: its height to be sixty cubits and its width sixty cubits, with three courses of hewn stone and one course of new native timber; the cost to be paid from the treasury of King Cyrus;

text.DRB: And the sacred vessels of the house of the Lord, as well of gold as of silver, which Nabuchodonosor took from the house of our Lord, which is in Jerusalem where they were laid, that they be put there:
text.KJV: 6:26 And that the holy vessels of the house of the Lord, both of gold and siluer that Nabuchodonosor tooke out of the house at Ierusalem, and brought to Babylon, should be restored to the house at Ierusalem, and bee set in the place where they were before.
text.NRSV: and that the holy vessels of the house of the Lord, both of gold and of silver, which Nebuchadnezzar took out of the house in Jerusalem and carried away to Babylon, should be restored to the house in Jerusalem, to be placed where they had been.’

text.DRB: And he commanded Sisennes the deputy of Syria & Phoenice, and Satrabuzanes, and his fellows, & them that were ordained presidents in Syria & Phoenice, that they should refrain themselves from that place.
text.KJV: 6:27 And also he commanded that Sisinnes the gouernour of Syria and Phenice, and Sathrabuzanes, and their companions, and those which were appointed rulers in Syria, and Phenice should be carefull not to meddle with the place, but suffer Zorobabel the seruant of the Lord, and gouernour of Iudea, and the Elders of the Iewes, to build the house of the Lord in that place.
text.NRSV: So Darius] commanded Sisinnes the governor of Syria and Phoenicia, and Sathrabuzanes, and their associates, and those who were appointed as local rulers in Syria and Phoenicia, to keep away from the place, and to permit Zerubbabel, the servant of the Lord and governor of Judea, and the elders of the Jews to build this house of the Lord on its site.

text.DRB: And I also have given commandment to build it wholly: & have provided, that they help them, which are of the captivity of the Jews, till the temple of the house of the Lord be accomplished.
text.KJV: 6:28 I haue commanded also to haue it built vp whole againe, and that they looke diligently to helpe those that be of the captiuitie of the Iewes, till the house of the Lord be finished.
text.NRSV: ‘And I command that it be built completely, and that full effort be made to help those who have returned from the exile of Judea, until the house of the Lord is finished;

text.DRB: And from the vexation of the tributes of Coelesyria & Phoenice, a quantity to be given diligently to these men for the sacrifice of the Lord, to Zorobabel the governor, for oxen, and rams, and lambs.
text.KJV: 6:29 And out of the tribute of Coelosyria, and Phenice, a portion carefully to be giuen these men, for the sacrifices of the Lord that is, to Zorobabel the gouernour, for bullocks, and rammes, and lambes;
text.NRSV: and that out of the tribute of Coelesyria and Phoenicia a portion be scrupulously given to these men, that is, to Zerubbabel the governor, for sacrifices to the Lord, for bulls and rams and lambs,

text.DRB: And in like manner corn also, and salt, and wine, and oil continually year by year, according as the Priests which are in Jerusalem, have prescribed to be spent daily:
text.KJV: 6:30 And also corne, salt, wine and oile, and that continually euery yeere without further question, according as the Priests that be in Ierusalem shall signifie, to be daily spent:
text.NRSV: and likewise wheat and salt and wine and oil, regularly every year, without quibbling, for daily use as the priests in Jerusalem may indicate,

text.DRB: that libaments may be offered to the most high God for the king & his children, & that they may pray for their life.
text.KJV: 6:31 That offrings may be made to the most high God, for the king and for his children, and that they may pray for their liues.
text.NRSV: in order that libations may be made to the Most High God for the king and his children, and prayers be offered for their lives.’

text.DRB: And that it be denounced, that whosoever shall transgress any thing of these which are written, or shall despise it, a beam be taken of their own, & they be hanged, & their goods be confiscate to the king.
text.KJV: 6:32 And he commanded, that whosoeuer should transgresse, yea, or make light of any thing afore spoken or written, out of his owne house should a tree be taken, and he thereon be hanged, and all his goods seized for the king.
text.NRSV: He commanded that if anyone should transgress or nullify any of the things herein written,] a beam should be taken out of the house of the perpetrator, who then should be impaled upon it, and all property forfeited to the king.

text.KJV: 6:33 The Lord therfore whose Name is there called vpon, vtterly destroy euery king and nation, that stretcheth out his hand to hinder or endammage that house of the Lord in Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: The king also gave orders that the temple should be finished, and that they should be careful to offer sacrifices there.
text.DRB: Therefore the Lord also, whose name is invocated there, destroy every king & nation, that shall extend their hand to hinder or to handle ill the house of the Lord which is in Jerusalem.
I Darius the king have decreed that it be most diligently done according to these things.
text.KJV: 6:34 I Darius the king haue ordeined, that according vnto these things it be done with diligence.
text.NRSV: ‘I, King Darius, have decreed that it be done with all diligence as here prescribed.’

text.KJV: 7:1 Then Sisinnes the gouernour of Coelosyria, and Phenice, and Sathrabuzanes, with their companions, following the commandements of king Darius,
text.NRSV: Then Sisinnes the governor of Greater Syria and Phoenicia and Sathrabuzanes and their associates, following the orders of King Darius,
text.DRB: Then Sisennes the deputy of Coelesyria, and Phenice, and Satrabuzanes, and their fellows, obeying those things which were decreed of Darius the king

text.KJV: 7:2 Did very carefully ouersee the holy workes, assisting the ancients of the Iewes, & gouernours of the Temple.
text.NRSV: supervised the holy work with very great care, assisting the elders of the Jews and the chief officers of the temple.
text.DRB: applied the sacred works most diligently, working together with the ancients of the Jews, the Princes of Syria.
And the sacred works prospered, Aggeus & Zacharias the Prophets prophesying.
text.KJV: 7:3 And so the holy workes prospered, when Aggeus, and Zacharias the Prophets prophecied.
text.NRSV: The holy work prospered, while the prophets Haggai and Zechariah prophesied;

text.DRB: And they accomplished all things by the precept of our Lord the God of Israel, and by the counsel of Cyrus, & Darius, and Artaxerxes the king of the Persians.
text.KJV: 7:4 And they finished these things, by the commandement of the Lord God of Israel, and with the consent of Cyrus, Darius, and Artaxerxes, kings of Persia.
text.NRSV: and they completed it by the command of the Lord God of Israel. So with the consent of Cyrus and Darius and Artaxerxes, kings of the Persians,

text.KJV: 7:5 And thus was the holy house finished, in the three and twentieth day of the moneth Adar, in the sixt yeere of Darius king of the Persians.
text.NRSV: The holy house was finished by the twenty-third day of the month of Adar, in the sixth year of King Darius.
text.DRB: And our house was a finishing until the three and twentieth day of the month of Adar, the sixth year of Darius the king.
And the children of Israel, and the Priests and Levites, and the rest that were of the captivity, which were added, did according to those things that are written in the book of Moyses.
text.KJV: 7:6 And the children of Israel: the Priests, and the Leuites, and other that were of the captiuitie, that were added vnto them, did according to the things written in the booke of Moses.
text.NRSV: And the people of Israel, the priests, the Levites, and the rest of those who returned from exile who joined them, did according to what was written in the book of Moses.

text.DRB: And they offered for the dedication of the temple of our Lord, oxen an hundred, rams two hundred, lambs four hundred.
text.KJV: 7:7 And to the dedication of the Temple of the Lord, they offered an hundred bullockes, two hundred rammes, foure hundred lambes;
text.NRSV: They offered at the dedication of the temple of the Lord one hundred bulls, two hundred rams, four hundred lambs,

text.DRB: And kids for the sins of all Israel, twelve, according to the number of the tribes of Israel.
text.KJV: 7:8 And twelue goats for the sinne of all Israel, according to the number of the chiefe of the tribes of Israel.
text.NRSV: and twelve male goats for the sin of all Israel, according to the number of the twelve leaders of the tribes of Israel;

text.DRB: And the Priests & Levites stood clothed with stoles by tribes, over all the works of our Lord the God of Israel, according to the book of Moyses, & the porters at every gate.
text.KJV: 7:9 The Priests also and the Leuites, stood arayed in their vestments according to their kinreds, in the seruices of the Lord God of Israel, according to the booke of Moses: and the porters at euery gate.
text.NRSV: and the priests and the Levites stood arrayed in their vestments, according to kindred, for the services of the Lord God of Israel in accordance with the book of Moses; and the gatekeepers were at each gate.

text.DRB: And the children of Israel, with them that were of the captivity, celebrated the phase the fourteenth moon of the first month, when the Priests & Levites were sanctified.
text.KJV: 7:10 And the children of Israel that were of the captiuitie, held the Passeouer the fourteenth day of the first moueth, after that the Priests and the Leuites were sanctified.
text.NRSV: The people of Israel who came from exile kept the passover on the fourteenth day of the first month, after the priests and the Levites were purified together.

text.DRB: All the children of the captivity were not sanctified together, because all the Levites were sanctified together.
text.KJV: 7:11 They that were of the captiuitie were not all sanctified together: but the Leuites were all sanctified together,
text.NRSV: Not all of the returned captives were purified, but the Levites were all purified together,]

text.DRB: And all the children of the captivity immolated the phase, both for their brethren the Priests, & for themselves.
text.KJV: 7:12 And so they offered the Passeouer for all them of the captiuitie, and for their brethren the Priestes, and for themselues.
text.NRSV: and they sacrificed the passover lamb for all the returned captives and for their kindred the priests and for themselves.

text.DRB: And the children of Israel did eat, they that were of the captivity, all that remained apart from all the abominations of the nations of the land seeking our Lord.
text.KJV: 7:13 And the children of Israel that came out of the captiuitie, did eate, euen all they that had separated themselues from the abominations of the people of the land, and sought the Lord.
text.NRSV: The people of Israel who had returned from exile ate it, all those who had separated themselves from the abominations of the peoples of the land and sought the Lord.

text.DRB: And they celebrated the festival day of Azymes seven days feasting in the sight of our Lord.
text.KJV: 7:14 And they kept the feast of vnleauened bread seuen dayes, making mercy before the Lord,
text.NRSV: They also kept the festival of unleavened bread seven days, rejoicing before the Lord,

text.DRB: Because he turned the counsel of the king of the Assirians toward them, to strengthen their hands to the works of our Lord the God of Israel.
text.KJV: 7:15 For that he had turned the counsell of the King of Assyria towards them to strengthen their hands in the workes of the Lord God of Israel.
text.NRSV: because he had changed the will of the king of the Assyrians concerning them, to strengthen their hands for the service of the Lord God of Israel.

text.KJV: 8:1 And after these things, when Artaxerxes the king of the Persians reigned, came Esdras the sonne of Saraias, the sonne of Ezerias, the sonne of Helchiah, the sonne of Salum,
text.NRSV: After these things, when Artaxerxes the king of the Persians was reigning, Ezra came... a scribe skilled in the law of Moses.
text.DRB: And after him when Artaxerxes king of the Persians reigned, came Esdras the son of Azarias, the son of Helcias the son of Salome
the son of Sadoc, the son of Achitob, the son of Ameri, the son of Azahel, the son of Bocci, the son of Abisue, the son of Phinees the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the first priest.
text.KJV: 8:2 The sonne of Sadduc, the sonne of Achitob, the sonne of Amarias, the sonne of Ozias, the sonne of Memeroth, the sonne of Zaraias, the sonne of Sauias, the sonne of Boccas, the sonne of Abisum, the sonne of Phinees, the sonne of Eleasar, the sonne of Aaron the chiefe Priest.
text.NRSV: son of Zadok, son of Ahitub, son of Amariah, son of Uzzi, son of Bukki, son of Abishua, son of Phineas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the high] priest.

text.DRB: This Esdras came up from Babylon being scribe & wise in the law of Moyses, which was given of our Lord the God of Israel to teach and to do.
text.KJV: 8:3 This Esdras went vp from Babylon, as a Scribe being very ready in the Law of Moyses, that was giuen by the God of Israel,
text.NRSV: This Ezra came up from Babylon as a scribe skilled in the law of Moses, which was given by the God of Israel;

text.DRB: And the king gave him glory, because he had found grace in all dignity and desire in his sight.
text.KJV: 8:4 And the king did him honour: for he found grace in his sight in all his requests.
text.NRSV: and the king showed him honour, for he found favour before the king] in all his requests.

text.DRB: And there went up with him of the children of Israel, and the Priests, and the Levites, and the sacred singers of the temple, and the porters, and the servants of the temple into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:5 There went vp with him also certaine of the children of Israel, of the Priests, of the Leuites, of the holy Singers, Porters, and Ministers of the Temple, vnto Ierusalem,
text.NRSV: There came up with him to Jerusalem some of the people of Israel and some of the priests and Levites and temple singers and gatekeepers and temple servants,

text.DRB: In the seventh year when Artaxerxes reigned in the fifth month, this is the seventh year of his reign, going forth of Babylon in the new moon of the fifth month,
text.KJV: 8:6 In the seuenth yere of the reigne of king Artaxerxes, in the fifth moneth, (this was the kings seuenth yeere) for they went from Babylon in the first day of the first moneth, and came to Ierusalem, according to the prosperous iourney which the Lord gaue them.
text.NRSV: in the seventh year of the reign of Artaxerxes, in the fifth month (this was the king’s seventh year); for they left Babylon at the new moon of the first month and arrived in Jerusalem at the new moon of the fifth month, by the prosperous journey that the Lord gave them.]

text.KJV: 8:7 For Esdras had very great skill, so that he omitted nothing of the Law and Commaundements of the Lord, but taught all Israel the Ordinances and Iudgements.
text.NRSV: For Ezra had great knowledge, so that he omitted nothing from the law of the Lord or the commandments, but taught all Israel all the ordinances and judgments.
text.DRB: they came to Jerusalem according to his commandment, according to the prosperity of their journey, which their Lord gave them.

For in these Esdras had great knowledge, that he would not pretermit any of those things, which were according to the law, and the precepts of our Lord, and in teaching all Israel all justice and judgement.
text.KJV: 8:8 Now the copy of the Commission which was written from Artaxerxes the King, and came to Esdras the priest and reader of the Law of the Lord, is this that followeth.
text.NRSV: The following is a copy of the written commission from King Artaxerxes that was delivered to Ezra the priest and reader of the law of the Lord:

text.DRB: And they that wrote the writings of Artaxerxes the king, coming delivered the writing which was granted of Artaxerxes the king to Esdras the Priest, & the reader of the law of our Lord, the copy whereof here followeth.
text.KJV: 8:9 King Artaxerxes vnto Esdras the Priest and reader of the Law of the Lord, sendeth greeting.
text.NRSV: ‘King Artaxerxes to Ezra the priest and reader of the law of the Lord, greeting.

text.DRB: KING Artaxerxes to Esdras the Priest, and reader of the law of the Lord, greeting.
text.KJV: 8:10 Hauing determined to deale graciously, I haue giuen order, that such of the nation of the Iewes, and of the Priests and Leuites being within our Realme, as are willing and desirous, should goe with thee vnto Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: In accordance with my gracious decision, I have given orders that those of the Jewish nation and of the priests and Levites and others in our realm, those who freely choose to do so, may go with you to Jerusalem.

text.DRB: I of courtesy esteeming it among benefits, have commanded them that of their own accord are desirous of the nation of the Jews, and of the Priests & Levites, which are in my kingdom, to go with thee into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:11 As many therefore as haue a minde thereunto, let them depart with thee, as it hath seemed good both to me, & my seuen friends the counsellors,
text.NRSV: Let as many as are so disposed, therefore, leave with you, just as I and the seven Friends who are my counsellors have decided,

text.DRB: If any therefore desire to go with thee, let them come together, and set forward as it hath pleased me, & my seven friends my counsellers:
text.KJV: 8:12 That they may looke vnto the affaires of Iudea and Ierusalem, agreeably to that which is in the Law of the Lord.
text.NRSV: in order to look into matters in Judea and Jerusalem, in accordance with what is in the law of the Lord,

text.DRB: that they may visit those things which are done touching Jury & Jerusalem, observing as thou hast in the law of the Lord.
text.KJV: 8:13 And cary the gifts vnto the Lord of Israel to Ierusalem, which I and my friends haue vowed, and all the golde and siluer that in the countrey of Babylon can be found, to the Lord in Ierusalem,
text.NRSV: and to carry to Jerusalem the gifts for the Lord of Israel that I and my Friends have vowed, and to collect for the Lord in Jerusalem all the gold and silver that may be found in the country of Babylonia,

text.DRB: And let them carry the gifts to the Lord the God of Israel, which I have vowed and my friends to Jerusalem, and all the gold and silver, that shall be found in the country of Babylon to the Lord in Jerusalem, with that,
text.KJV: 8:14 With that also which is giuen of the people, for the Temple of the Lord their God at Ierusalem: and that siluer and golde may be collected for bullocks, rammes and lambes, and things thereunto appertaining,
text.NRSV: together with what is given by the nation for the temple of their Lord that is in Jerusalem, both gold and silver, for bulls and rams and lambs and what goes with them,

text.DRB: which is given for the nation itself unto the temple of their Lord which is in Jerusalem: that this gold and silver be gathered for oxen, and rams, and lambs, & kids, & for the things that are agreeable to these,
text.KJV: 8:15 To the end that they may offer sacrifices vnto the Lord, vpon the Altar of the Lord their God, which is in Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: so as to offer sacrifices on the altar of their Lord that is in Jerusalem.

text.DRB: that they may offer hosts to the Lord upon the altar of their Lord, which is in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:16 And whatsoeuer thou and thy brethren will doe with the siluer and golde, that doe according to the will of thy God.
text.NRSV: Whatever you and your kindred are minded to do with the gold and silver, perform it in accordance with the will of your God;

text.DRB: And all things whatsoever thou with thy brethren wilt do with gold, & silver, do it at thy pleasure, according to the precept of the Lord thy God.
text.KJV: 8:17 And the holy vessels of the Lord which are giuen thee, for the vse of the Temple of thy God which is in Ierusalem, thou shalt set before thy God in Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: deliver the holy vessels of the Lord that are given to you for the use of the temple of your God that is in Jerusalem.

text.DRB: And the sacred vessels, which are given thee to the works of the house of the Lord thy God, which is in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:18 And whatsoeuer thing else thou shalt remember for the vse of the Temple of thy God, thou shalt giue it out of the kings treasury.
text.NRSV: And whatever else occurs to you as necessary for the temple of your God, you may provide out of the royal treasury.

text.DRB: And other things whatsoever shall help thee to the works of the temple of thy God, thou shalt give it out of the king's treasure.
text.KJV: 8:19 And I, king Artaxerxes, haue also commaunded the keepers of the treasures in Syria and Phenice, that whatsoeuer Esdras the priest, and the reader of the law of the most high God shall send for, they should giue it him with speed,
text.NRSV: ‘I, King Artaxerxes, have commanded the treasurers of Syria and Phoenicia that whatever Ezra the priest and reader of the law of the Most High God sends for, they shall take care to give him,

text.DRB: When thou with thy brethren wilt do ought with gold and silver, do according to the will of the Lord.
text.KJV: 8:20 To the summe of an hundred talents of siluer: likewise also of wheat euen to an hundred cores, and an hundred pieces of wine, and other things in abundance.
text.NRSV: up to a hundred talents of silver, and likewise up to a hundred cors of wheat, a hundred baths of wine, and salt in abundance.

text.DRB: And I king Artaxerxes have given commandment to the keepers of the treasure of Syria and Phenice, that what things soever Esdras the Priest and reader of the law of the Lord shall write for, they give him unto an hundred talents of silver, likewise also of gold.
text.KJV: 8:21 Let all things be performed after the law of God diligently vnto the most high God, that wrath come not vpon the kingdome of the King and his sonnes.
text.NRSV: Let all things prescribed in the law of God be scrupulously fulfilled for the Most High God, so that wrath may not come upon the kingdom of the king and his sons.

text.DRB: And unto an hundred measures of corn, & an hundred vessels of wine, and other things whatsoever abound without taxing.
text.KJV: 8:22 I command you also that yee require no taxe, nor any other imposition of any of the Priests or Leuites, or holy singers, or porters, or ministers of the temple, or of any that haue doings in this temple, and that no man haue authority to impose any thing vpon them.
text.NRSV: You are also informed that no tribute or any other tax is to be laid on any of the priests or Levites or temple singers or gatekeepers or temple servants or persons employed in this temple, and that no one has authority to impose any tax on them.

text.DRB: Let all things be done to the most high God according to the law of God, lest perhaps there arise wrath in the reign of the king, & of his son, & his sons.
text.KJV: 8:23 And thou, Esdras, according to the wisedome of God, ordaine iudges, and iustices, that they may iudge in all Syria and Phenice, all those that know the law of thy God, and those that know it not thou shalt teach.
text.NRSV: ‘And you, Ezra, according to the wisdom of God, appoint judges and justices to judge all those who know the law of your God, throughout all Syria and Phoenicia; and you shall teach it to those who do not know it.

text.DRB: And to you it is said, that upon all the Priests, & Levites, & sacred singers, & servants of the temple, and scribes of this temple
text.KJV: 8:24 And whosoeuer shal transgresse the law of thy God, and of the king, shall be punished diligently, whether it be by death or other punishment, by penalty of money, or by imprisonment.
text.NRSV: All who transgress the law of your God or the law of the kingdom shall be strictly punished, whether by death or some other punishment, either fine or imprisonment.’

text.KJV: 8:25 ¶ Then said Esdras the Scribe, Blessed be the onely Lord God of my fathers, who hath put these things into the heart of the king, to glorifie his house that is in Ierusalem;
text.NRSV: “And you, Ezra, according to the wisdom of God, appoint judges and justices to judge all those who know the law of your God throughout all Syria and Phoenicia.”
text.DRB: no tribute, nor any other tax be set, & that no man have authority to object any
But thou Esdras according to the wisdom of God appoint judges, and arbiters in all Syria and Phenice: and teach all them that know not the law of thy God:
text.KJV: 8:26 And hath honoured mee in the sight of the king and his counsellers, and all his friends and Nobles.
text.NRSV: and who honoured me in the sight of the king and his counsellors and all his Friends and nobles.

text.DRB: that whosoever shall transgress the law, they be diligently punished either with death, or with torment, or else with a forfeit of money, or with banishment.
text.KJV: 8:27 Therefore was I encouraged, by the helpe of the Lord my God, and gathered together men of Israel to goe vp with me:
text.NRSV: I was encouraged by the help of the Lord my God, and I gathered men from Israel to go up with me.’

text.DRB: And Esdras the scribe said: Blessed be the God of our fathers, which hath given this will into the king's heart, to glorify his house, which is in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:28 And these are the chiefe according to their families and seuerall dignities, that went vp with me from Babylon in the reigne of king Artaxerxes.
text.NRSV: These are the leaders, according to their ancestral houses and their groups, who went up with me from Babylon, in the reign of King Artaxerxes:

text.DRB: And hath honoured me in the sight of the king, and of his counsellers, and friends, and them that wear purple.
text.KJV: 8:29 Of the sonnes of Phinees, Gerson: of the sonnes of Ithamar, Gamael: of the sonnes of Dauid; Lettus the sonne of Sechenias:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Phineas, Gershom. Of the descendants of Ithamar, Gamael. Of the descendants of David, Hattush son of Shecaniah.

text.DRB: And I was made constant in mind according to the aid of our Lord my God, and gathered together of Israel men, that should go up together with me.
text.KJV: 8:30 Of the sonnes of Pharez, Zacharias, and with him were counted, an hundred and fifty men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Parosh, Zechariah, and with him a hundred and fifty men enrolled.

text.DRB: And these are the Princes according to their kindreds, and several principalities of them that came up from Babylon the Kingdom of Artaxerxes.
text.KJV: 8:31 Of the sonnes of Pahath, Moab; Eliaonias, the sonne of Zaraias, and with him two hundred men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Pahath-moab, Eliehoenai son of Zerahiah, and with him two hundred men.

text.DRB: Of the children of Phares, Gersomus: and of the children of Siemarith, Amenus: of the children of David, Acchus the son of Scecilia:
text.KJV: 8:32 Of the sonnes of Zathoe, Sechenias, the sonne of Iezelus, and with him three hundred men, Of the sonnes of Adin, Obeth the sonne of Ionathan, and with him two hundred and fifty men.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Zattu, Shecaniah son of Jahaziel, and with him three hundred men. Of the descendants of Adin, Obed son of Jonathan, and with him two hundred and fifty men.

text.DRB: Of the children of Phares, Zacharias, and with him returned an hundred fifty men.
text.KJV: 8:33 Of the sonnes of Elam, Iosias sonne of Gotholias, and with him seuenty men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Elam, Jeshaiah son of Gotholiah, and with him seventy men.

text.DRB: Of the children of leader Moabilion, Zaraei, and with him two hundred fifty men.
text.KJV: 8:34 Of the sonnes of Saphatias, Zaraias sonne of Michael, and with him threescore and ten men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Shephatiah, Zeraiah son of Michael, and with him seventy men.

text.DRB: Of the children of Zachues, Iechonias of Zechoel, and with him two hundred fifty men:
text.KJV: 8:35 Of the sonnes of Ioab, Abadias sonne of Iezelus, and with him two hundred and twelue men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Joab, Obadiah son of Jehiel, and with him two hundred and twelve men.

text.DRB: of the children of Sala, Maasias of Gotholia, & with him seventy men:
text.KJV: 8:36 Of the sonnes of Banid, Assalimoth sonne of Iosaphias, and with him an hundred and threescore men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bani, Shelomith son of Josiphiah, and with him a hundred and sixty men.

text.DRB: of the children of Saphatia, Zarias of Michel, and with him eighty men:
text.KJV: 8:37 Of the sonnes of Babi, Zacharias sonne of Bebai, and with him twentie and eight men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bebai, Zechariah son of Bebai, and with him twenty-eight men.

text.DRB: of the children of Iob, Abdias of Iehel, and with him two hundred twelve men:
text.KJV: 8:38 Of the sonnes of Astath, Iohannessonne of Acatan, and with him an hundred and ten men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Azgad, Johanan son of Hakkatan, and with him a hundred and ten men.

text.DRB: of the children of Bania, Salimoth, the son of Iosaphia, and with him an hundred sixty men:
text.KJV: 8:39 Of the sonnes of Adonicam the last, and these are the names of them, Eliphalet, Ieuel, and Samaias and with them seuenty men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Adonikam, the last ones, their names being Eliphelet, Jeuel, and Shemaiah, and with them seventy men.

text.DRB: of the children of Beer, Zacharias Bebei, and with him two hundred eight men:
text.KJV: 8:40 Of the sonnes of Bago, Uthi, the sonne of Istalcurus, and with him seuenty men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bigvai, Uthai son of Istalcurus, and with him seventy men.

text.DRB: of the children of Ezead, Ioannes of Eccetan, and with him an hundred ten men:
text.KJV: 8:41 And these I gathered together to the riuer, called Theras, where we pitched our tents three dayes, and then I suruayed them.
text.NRSV: I assembled them at the river called Theras, and we encamped there for three days, and I inspected them.

text.DRB: of the children of Adonicam, which were last, and these are their names, Eliphalam the son of Gebel, and Semeias, and with him seventy men.
text.KJV: 8:42 But when I had found there, none of the priests and Leuites,
text.NRSV: When I found there none of the descendants of the priests or of the Levites,

text.DRB: And I gathered them together to the river that is called Thia, and we camped there three days, and viewed them again.
text.KJV: 8:43 Then sent I vnto Eleazar and Iduel, and Masman,
text.NRSV: I sent word to Eliezar, Iduel, Maasmas,

text.DRB: And of the children of the Priests and Levites I found not there.
text.KJV: 8:44 And Alnathan, and Mamaias, and Ioribas, and Nathan, Eunatan, Zacharias, and Mosollamon principal men and learned.
text.NRSV: Elnathan, Shemaiah, Jarib, Nathan, Elnathan, Zechariah, and Meshullam, who were leaders and men of understanding;

text.DRB: And I sent to Eleazarus, and Eccelon, and Masman, and Maloban, and Enaathan, and Samea, and Ioribum, Nathan, Enuagam, Zacharias, and Mosolam the leaders themselves, and that were skillful.
text.KJV: 8:45 And I bad them that they should goe vnto Saddeus the captaine, who was in the place of the treasury:
text.NRSV: I told them to go to Iddo, who was the leading man at the place of the treasury,

text.DRB: And I said to them that they should come to Loddeus, who was at the place of the treasury.
text.KJV: 8:46 And commanded them that they should speake vnto Daddeus, and to his brethren, and to the treasurers in that place, to send vs such men as might execute the Priests office in the house of the Lord.
text.NRSV: and ordered them to tell Iddo and his kindred and the treasurers at that place to send us men to serve as priests in the house of our Lord.

text.DRB: And I commanded them to say to Loddeus, and his brethren, and to them that were in the treasury, that they should send us them that might do the function of priesthood in the house of the Lord our God.
text.KJV: 8:47 And by the mighty hand of our Lord they brought vnto vs skilful men of the sonnes of Moli, the sonne of Leui, the sonne of Israel, Asebebia and his sonnes and his brethren, who were eighteene.
text.NRSV: And by the mighty hand of our Lord they brought us competent men of the descendants of Mahli son of Levi, son of Israel, namely Sherebiah] with his descendants and kinsmen, eighteen;

text.DRB: And they brought unto us according to the mighty hand of the Lord our God cunning men: of the children of Moholi, the son of Levi, the son of Israel, Sebebia, & his sons and brethren, which were eighteen:
text.KJV: 8:48 And Asebia, and Annuus, and Osaias his brother of the sonnes of Channuneus, and their sonnes were twentie men.
text.NRSV: also Hashabiah and Annunus and his brother Jeshaiah, of the descendants of Hananiah, and their descendants, twenty men;

text.DRB: Asbia, and Amin of the sons of the children of Chananeus, and their children twenty men.
text.KJV: 8:49 And of the seruants of the Temple whom Dauid had ordeined, and the principall men, for the seruice of the Leuites (to wit) the seruants of the Temple, two hundred and twentie, the catalogue of whose names were shewed.
text.NRSV: and of the temple servants, whom David and the leaders had given for the service of the Levites, two hundred and twenty temple servants; the list of all their names was reported.

text.DRB: And of them that served the temple, whom David gave, and the princes themselves to the ministry of the Levites of them that served the temple, two hundred twenty. All their names were signified in writings.
text.KJV: 8:50 And there I vowed a fast vnto the yong men before our Lord, to desire of him a prosperous iourney, both for vs, and them that were with vs: for our children and for the cattell:
text.NRSV: There I proclaimed a fast for the young men before our Lord, to seek from him a prosperous journey for ourselves and for our children and the livestock that were with us.

text.DRB: And I vowed there a fast to the young men in the sight of God, that I might ask of him a good journey for us, and them that were with us, and for the children, and the cattle because of ambushments.
text.KJV: 8:51 For I was ashamed to aske the king footmen, & horsemen, and conduct for safegard against our aduersaries:
text.NRSV: For I was ashamed to ask the king for foot-soldiers and cavalry and an escort to keep us safe from our adversaries;

text.DRB: For I was ashamed to ask of the king footmen and horsemen in my company, to guard us, against our adversaries.
text.KJV: 8:52 For wee had said vnto the king, that the power of the Lord our God, should be with them that seeke him, to support them in all wayes.
text.NRSV: for we had said to the king, ‘The power of our Lord will be with those who seek him, and will support them in every way.’

text.DRB: For we said to the king that the power of our Lord will be with them that seek him with all affection.
text.KJV: 8:53 And againe wee besought our Lord, as touching these things, & found him fauourable vnto vs.
text.NRSV: And again we prayed to our Lord about these things, and we found him very merciful.

text.DRB: And again we besought the Lord our God according to these things: whom also we had propitious, and we obtained of our God.
text.KJV: 8:54 Then I separated twelue of the chiefe of the priests, Esebrias, & Assanias, and ten men of their brethren with them.
text.NRSV: Then I set apart twelve of the leaders of the priests, Sherebiah and Hashabiah, and ten of their kinsmen with them;

text.DRB: And I separated of the rulers of the people, and of the Priests of the temple, twelve men, and Sedebia, and Asanna, and with them of their brethren ten men.
text.KJV: 8:55 And I weighed them the golde, and the siluer, and the holy vessels of the house of our Lord, which the king and his counsell, and the princes, and all Israel had giuen.
text.NRSV: and I weighed out to them the silver and the gold and the holy vessels of the house of our Lord, which the king himself and his counsellors and the nobles and all Israel had given.

text.DRB: And I weighed to them the gold and silver, and the vessels of the house of our God pertaining to the Priests, which the king had given, and his counsellers, and the princes, and all Israel.
text.KJV: 8:56 And when I had weighed it, I deliuered vnto them sixe hundred and fiftie talents of siluer, and siluer vessels of an hundred talents, and an hundred talents of gold,
text.NRSV: I weighed and gave to them six hundred and fifty talents of silver, and silver vessels worth a hundred talents, and a hundred talents of gold,

text.DRB: And when I had weighed it, I delivered of silver an hundred fifty talents, and silver vessels of an hundred talents, and of gold an hundred talents.
text.KJV: 8:57 And twentie golden vessels, and twelue vessels of brasse, euen of fine brasse, glittering like gold.
text.NRSV: and twenty golden bowls, and twelve bronze vessels of fine bronze that glittered like gold.

text.DRB: And of vessels of gold seven score and twelve brazen vessels good of shining brass, resembling the form of gold.
text.KJV: 8:58 And I said vnto them, Both you are holy vnto the Lord, and the vessels are holy, and the golde, and the siluer isa vowe vnto the Lord, the Lord of our fathers.
text.NRSV: And I said to them, ‘You are holy to the Lord, and the vessels are holy, and the silver and the gold are vowed to the Lord, the Lord of our ancestors.

text.DRB: And I said to them: You are also sanctified to our Lord, and the vessels be holy, and the gold and silver is vowed to our Lord the God of our fathers.
text.KJV: 8:59 Watch ye, and keepe them till yee deliuer them to the chiefe of the priestes and Leuites, and to the principall men of the families of Israel in Ierusalem into the chambers of the house of our God.
text.NRSV: Be watchful and on guard until you deliver them to the leaders of the priests and the Levites, and to the heads of the ancestral houses of Israel, in Jerusalem, in the chambers of the house of our Lord.’

text.DRB: And those Priests and Levites that received the gold and silver and vessels, brought it to Jerusalem into the temple of our Lord.
text.KJV: 8:61 And from the riuer Theras wee departed the twelft day of the first moneth, and came to Ierusalem by the mightie hand of our Lord, which was with vs: and from the beginning of our iourney, the Lord deliuered vs from euery enemy, and so wee came to Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: We left the river Theras on the twelfth day of the first month; and we arrived in Jerusalem by the mighty hand of our Lord, which was upon us; he delivered us from every enemy on the way, and so we came to Jerusalem.

text.DRB: And we went forward from the river Thia, the twelfth day of the first month, till we entered into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:62 And when wee had bene there three dayes, the golde and siluer that was weighed, was deliuered in the house of our Lord on the fourth day vnto Marmoth the priest, the sonne of Iri.
text.NRSV: When we had been there for three days, the silver and the gold were weighed and delivered in the house of our Lord to the priest Meremoth son of Uriah;

text.DRB: And when the third day was come, in the fourth day the gold being weighed, and the silver, was delivered in the house of the Lord our God, to Marimoth Priest the son of Iori.
text.KJV: 8:63 And with him was Eleazar the sonne of Phinees, and with them were Iosabad the sonne of Iesu, and Moeth the sonne of Sabban, Leuites: all was deliuered them by number and weight.
text.NRSV: with him was Eleazar son of Phinehas, and with them were Jozabad son of Jeshua and Moeth son of Binnui,] the Levites.

text.DRB: And with him was Eleazar the son of Phinees: and with them were Iosadus the son of Iesus, and Medias, and Banni the son of a Levite, by number and weight all things.
text.KJV: 8:64 And all the weight of them was written vp the same houre.
text.NRSV: The whole was counted and weighed, and the weight of everything was recorded at that very time.

text.DRB: And the weight of them was written the same hour.
text.KJV: 8:65 Moreouer they that were come out of the captiuitie offered sacrifice vnto the Lord God of Israel, euen twelue bullocks for all Israel, fourescore and sixteene rammes,
text.NRSV: And those who had returned from exile offered sacrifices to the Lord, the God of Israel, twelve bulls for all Israel, ninety-six rams,

text.DRB: And they that came out of the captivity, offered sacrifice to our Lord the God of Israel, oxen twelve, for all Israel, rams eighty six,
text.KJV: 8:66 Threescore and twelue lambes, goates for a peace offering, twelue, all of them a sacrifice to the Lord.
text.NRSV: seventy-two lambs, and as a thank-offering twelve male goats—all as a sacrifice to the Lord.

text.DRB: lambs seventy two, buck goats for sin twelve, and for health twelve kine, all for the sacrifice of our Lord.
text.KJV: 8:67 And they deliuered the kings commandements vnto the kings stewards, and to the gouernours of Coelosyria, and Phenice, and they honoured the people, and the Temple of God.
text.NRSV: They delivered the king’s orders to the royal stewards and to the governors of Coelesyria and Phoenicia; and these officials] honoured the people and the temple of the Lord.

text.DRB: And they read again the precepts of the king to the kings' officers, and to the deputies of Coelesyria, and Phoenice: and they honored the nation, and the temple of our Lord.
text.KJV: 8:68 Now when these things were done, the rulers came vnto me, and said:
text.NRSV: After these things had been done, the leaders came to me and said,

text.DRB: And these things being finished, the rulers came to me, saying: The stock of Israel, and the princes, and the Priests, and the Levites
text.KJV: 8:69 The nation of Israel, the princes, the priests, and Leuites haue not put away from them the strange people of the land: nor the pollutions of the Gentiles, to wit, of the Chanaanites, Hittites, Pheresites, Iebusites, and the Moabites, Egyptians, and Edomites.
text.NRSV: ‘The people of Israel and the rulers and the priests and the Levites have not put away from themselves the alien peoples of the land and their pollutions, the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Jebusites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Edomites.

text.DRB: and the strange people, and nations of the land have not separated their uncleanness from the Chananeites, and Hetheites, and Pherezeites, and Iebuseites, and Moobites, & Ægyptians, and Idumeians.
text.KJV: 8:70 For both they, and their sonnes, haue maried with their daughters, and the holy seed is mixed with the strange people of the land, and from the beginning of this matter, the rulers and the great men haue bene partakers of this iniquitie.
text.NRSV: For they and their descendants have married the daughters of these people,] and the holy race has been mixed with the alien peoples of the land; and from the beginning of this matter the leaders and the nobles have been sharing in this iniquity.’

text.DRB: For they are joined to their daughters both themselves, and their sons: and the holy seed is mingled with the strange nations of the earth, and the rulers and magistrates were partakers of that iniquity from the beginning of the reign itself.
text.KJV: 8:71 And assoone as I had heard these things, I rent my clothes, and the holy garment, and pulled off the haire from off my head, and beard, and sate me downe sad, and very heauy.
text.NRSV: As soon as I heard these things I tore my garments and my holy mantle, and pulled out hair from my head and beard, and sat down in anxiety and grief.

text.DRB: And forthwith as I heard these things, I rent my garments and the sacred tunic: and tearing the hairs of my head, and my beard, I sat sorrowful and heavy.
text.KJV: 8:72 So all they that were then mooued at the word of the Lord God of Israel, assembled vnto me, whilest I mourned for the iniquitie: but I sate still full of heauinesse, vntill the euening sacrifice.
text.NRSV: And all who were ever moved at] the word of the Lord of Israel gathered around me, as I mourned over this iniquity, and I sat grief-stricken until the evening sacrifice.

text.DRB: And there assembled to me mourning upon this iniquity, as many as were then moved by the word of our Lord the God of Israel, and I sat sad until the evening sacrifice.
text.KJV: 8:73 Then rising vp from the fast with my clothes and the holy garment rent, and bowing my knees, and stretching foorth my hands vnto the Lord:
text.NRSV: Then I rose from my fast, with my garments and my holy mantle torn, and kneeling down and stretching out my hands to the Lord

text.DRB: And I rising up from fasting, having my garments rent and the sacred tunic, kneeling, and stretching forth my hands to our Lord,
text.KJV: 8:74 I said, O Lord, I am confounded, and ashamed before thy face;
text.NRSV: ‘O Lord, I am ashamed and confused before your face.

text.DRB: I said: Lord I am confounded, and ashamed before thy face,
text.KJV: 8:75 For our sinnes are multiplied aboue our heads, and our ignorances haue reached vp vnto heauen.
text.NRSV: For our sins have risen higher than our heads, and our mistakes have mounted up to heaven

text.DRB: for our sins are multiplied over our heads, and our iniquities are exalted even to heaven.
text.KJV: 8:76 For euer since the time of our fathers wee haue bene and are in great sinne, euen vnto this day:
text.NRSV: from the times of our ancestors, and we are in great sin to this day.

text.DRB: Because from the times of our fathers we are in great sin unto this day.
text.KJV: 8:77 And for our sinnes and our fathers, we with our brethren, and our kings, and our priests, were giuen vp vnto the Kings of the earth, to the sword, and to captiuitie, and for a pray with shame, vnto this day.
text.NRSV: Because of our sins and the sins of our ancestors, we with our kindred and our kings and our priests were given over to the kings of the earth, to the sword and exile and plundering, in shame until this day.

text.DRB: And for the sins of us, and of our fathers we have been delivered with our brethren, and with our Priests to the kings of the earth, into sword and captivity, and spoil with confusion unto this present day.
text.KJV: 8:78 And now in some measure hath mercy bene shewed vnto vs, from thee, O Lord, that there should be left vs a roote, and a name, in the place of thy Sanctuary.
text.NRSV: And now in some measure mercy has come to us from you, O Lord, to leave to us a root and a name in your holy place,

text.DRB: And now what a great thing is this that mercy hath happened to us from thee, o Lord God, & leave thou unto us a root, and a name in the place of thy sanctification,
text.KJV: 8:79 And to discouer vnto vs a light in the house of the Lord our God, and to giue vs foode in the time of our seruitude.
text.NRSV: and to uncover a light for us in the house of the Lord our God, and to give us food in the time of our servitude.

text.DRB: to discover our light in the house of the Lord our God, to give us meat in all the time of our bondage.
text.KJV: 8:80 Yea, when we were in bondage, we were not forsaken of our Lord; but he made vs gracious before the Kings of Persia, so that they gaue vs food;
text.NRSV: Even in our bondage we were not forsaken by our Lord, but he brought us into favour with the kings of the Persians, so that they have given us food

text.DRB: And when we served, we were not forsaken of the Lord our God: but he set us in favour, appointing the kings of the Persians to give us meat,
text.KJV: 8:81 Yea, and honoured the Temple of our Lord, and raised vp the desolate Sion, that they haue giuen vs a sure abiding in Iurie, and Ierusalem.
text.NRSV: and glorified the temple of our Lord, and raised Zion from desolation, to give us a stronghold in Judea and Jerusalem.

text.DRB: and to glorify the temple of the Lord our God, and to build the desolations of Sion, to give us stability in Jury, and Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:82 And now, O Lord, what shall wee say hauing these things? for wee haue transgressed thy Commaundements, which thou gauest by the hand of thy seruants the Prophets, saying,
text.NRSV: ‘And now, O Lord, what shall we say, when we have these things? For we have transgressed your commandments, which you gave by your servants the prophets, saying,

text.DRB: And now what say we Lord, having these things? For we have transgressed thy precepts, which thou gavest into the hands of thy servants the prophets,
text.KJV: 8:83 That the land which ye enter into to possesse as an heritage, is a land polluted with the pollutions of the strangers of the land, and they haue filled it with their vncleannesse.
text.NRSV: “The land that you are entering to take possession of is a land polluted with the pollution of the aliens of the land, and they have filled it with their uncleanness.

text.DRB: saying: That the land into which ye entered to possess the inheritance thereof, is a land polluted with the coinquinations of the strangers of the land, and their uncleanness hath filled it wholly with their filthiness.
text.KJV: 8:84 Therefore now shal ye not ioyne your daughters vnto their sonnes, neither shall ye take their daughters vnto your sonnes.
text.NRSV: Therefore do not give your daughters in marriage to their descendants, and do not take their daughters for your descendants;

text.DRB: And now your daughters you shall not match with their sons, and their daughters you shall not take for your sons.
text.KJV: 8:85 Moreouer you shall neuer seeke to haue peace with them, that yee may be strong, and eate the good things of the land, and that ye may leaue the inheritance of the land vnto your children for euermore.
text.NRSV: do not seek ever to have peace with them, so that you may be strong and eat the good things of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever.”

text.DRB: And you shall not seek to have peace with them for ever, that growing strong you may eat the best things of the land, and may distribute the inheritance to your children for ever.
text.KJV: 8:86 And all that is befallen, is done vnto vs for our wicked workes, and great sinnes: for thou, O Lord, didst make our sinnes light:
text.NRSV: And all that has happened to us has come about because of our evil deeds and our great sins. For you, O Lord, lifted the burden of our sins

text.DRB: And the things that happen to us, all are done for our naughty works, and our great sins.
text.KJV: 8:87 And didst giue vnto vs such a roote: but we haue turned backe againe to transgresse thy Law, and to mingle our selues with the vncleannesse of the nations of the land.
text.NRSV: and gave us such a root as this; but we turned back again to transgress your law by mixing with the uncleanness of the peoples of the land.

text.DRB: And thou gavest us such a root, and we are returned again to transgress thy ordinances, that we would be mingled with the uncleanness of the nations of this land.
text.KJV: 8:88 Mightest not thou be angry with vs to destroy vs, till thou hadst left vs neither root, seed, nor name?
text.NRSV: Were you not angry enough with us to destroy us without leaving a root or seed or name?

text.DRB: Wilt not thou be wrath with us to destroy us, till there be no root left nor our name?
text.KJV: 8:89 O Lord of Israel, thou art true: for we are left a root this day.
text.NRSV: O Lord of Israel, you are faithful; for we are left as a root to this day.

text.DRB: Lord God of Israel thou art true. For there is a root left until this present day.
text.KJV: 8:90 Behold, now are we before thee in our iniquities, for wee cannot stand any longer by reason of these things before thee.
text.NRSV: See, we are now before you in our iniquities; for we can no longer stand in your presence because of these things.’

text.KJV: 8:91 And as Esdras in his praier made his confession, weeping, and lying flat vpon the ground before the Temple, there gathered vnto him from Ierusalem, a very great multitude of men, and women, & children: for there was great weeping among the multitude.
text.NRSV: While Ezra was praying and making his confession, weeping and lying face down on the ground before the temple, a very great multitude of men, women, and children gathered around him from Jerusalem; for there was great weeping among the people.
text.DRB: Behold, now we are in

And when Esdras with adoration confessed weeping, lying flat on the ground before the temple, there were gathered before him out of Jerusalem a very great multitude, men and women, and young men and young women. For there was great weeping in the multitude itself.
text.KJV: 8:92 Then Iechonias the sonne of Ieelus, one of the sonnes of Israel called out and saide, O Esdras, wee haue sinned against the Lord God, wee haue maried strange women of the nations of the land, & now is all Israel aloft.
text.NRSV: Then Shecaniah son of Jehiel, one of the men of Israel, called out, and said to Ezra, ‘We have sinned against the Lord, and have married foreign women from the peoples of the land; but even now there is hope for Israel.

text.DRB: And when he had cried, Iechonias of Ieheli of the children of Israel, said to Esdras: We have sinned against our Lord, for that we have taken unto us in marriage strange women of the nations of the land.
text.KJV: 8:93 Let vs make an oath to the Lord, that wee will put away all our wiues, which we haue taken of the heathen, with their children,
text.NRSV: Let us take an oath to the Lord about this, that we will put away all our foreign wives, with their children,

text.DRB: And now thou art over all Israel, in these therefore let there be an oath from our Lord to expel all our wives that are of strangers with their children.
text.KJV: 8:94 Like as thou hast decreed, and as many as doe obey the Law of the Lord.
text.NRSV: as seems good to you and to all who obey the law of the Lord.

text.DRB: As it was decreed to thee of the ancesters according to the law of our Lord, rising up declare it.
text.KJV: 8:95 Arise, and put in execution: for to thee doeth this matter appertaine, and wee will bee with thee: doe valiantly.
text.NRSV: Rise up] and take action, for it is your task, and we are with you to take strong measures.’

text.DRB: For to thee the business pertaineth, and we are with thee: do manfully.
text.KJV: 8:96 So Esdras arose, and tooke an oath of the chiefe of the Priestes, and Leuites of all Israel, to do after these things, and so they sware.
text.NRSV: Then Ezra rose up and made the leaders of the priests and Levites of all Israel swear that they would do this. And they swore to it.

text.DRB: And Esdras rising up adjured the princes of the Priests and Levites, and all Israel to do according to these things and they sware.

text.KJV: 9:1 Then Esdras rising from the court of the Temple, went to the chamber of Ioanan the sonne of Eliasib,
text.NRSV: Then Ezra rose and went from the court of the temple to the chamber of Jehohanan son of Eliashib.
text.DRB: And Esdras rising up from before the court of the temple, went into the chamber of Ionathas the son of Nasabi.
And lodging there he tasted no bread, nor drank water for the iniquity of the multitude.
text.KJV: 9:2 And remained there, and did eate no meate nor drinke water, mourning for the great iniquities of the multitude.
text.NRSV: and spent the night there; and he did not eat bread or drink water, for he was mourning over the great iniquities of the multitude.

text.DRB: And there was proclamation made in all Jury, & in Jerusalem to all that were of the captivity gathered in Jerusalem,
text.KJV: 9:3 And there was a proclamation in all Iury and Ierusalem, to all them that were of the captiuitie, that they should be gathered together at Ierusalem:
text.NRSV: And a proclamation was made throughout Judea and Jerusalem to all who had returned from exile that they should assemble at Jerusalem,

text.DRB: that whosoever shall not appear with in two or three days, according to the judgement of the ancients sitting upon it, their goods should be taken away, and himself should be judged an alien from the multitude of the captivity.
text.KJV: 9:4 And that whosoeuer met not there within two or three dayes according as the Elders that bare rule, appointed, their cattell should be seized to the vse of the Temple, and himselfe cast out from them that were of the captiuitie.
text.NRSV: and that if any did not meet there within two or three days, in accordance with the decision of the ruling elders, their livestock would be seized for sacrifice and the men themselves] expelled from the multitude of those who had returned from the captivity.

text.DRB: And all were gathered that were of the tribe of Juda, and of Benjamin within three days in Jerusalem: this is the ninth month, the twentieth day of the month.
text.KJV: 9:5 And in three dayes were all they of the tribe of Iuda and Beniamin gathered together at Ierusalem the twentieth day of the ninth moneth.
text.NRSV: Then the men of the tribe of Judah and Benjamin assembled at Jerusalem within three days; this was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month.

text.DRB: And all the multitude sat in the court of the temple trembling, for the present winter.
text.KJV: 9:6 And all the multitude sate trembling in the broad court of the Temple, because of the present foule weather.
text.NRSV: All the multitude sat in the open square before the temple, shivering because of the bad weather that prevailed.

text.DRB: And Esdras rising up said to them: You have done unlawfully taking to you in marriage strange wives, that you might add to the sins of Israel.
text.KJV: 9:7 So Esdras arose vp, and said vnto them, Ye haue transgressed the law in marrying strange wiues, thereby to increase the sinnes of Israel.
text.NRSV: Then Ezra stood up and said to them, ‘You have broken the law and married foreign women, and so have increased the sin of Israel.

text.DRB: And now give confession, & magnificence to our Lord the God of our fathers:
text.KJV: 9:8 And now by confessing giue glory vnto the Lord God of our fathers,
text.NRSV: Now then, make confession and give glory to the Lord the God of our ancestors,

text.DRB: and accomplish his will, and depart from the nations of the land, and from your wives the strangers.
text.KJV: 9:9 And doe his will, and separate your selues from the heathen of the land, and from the strange women.
text.NRSV: and do his will; separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from your foreign wives.’

text.DRB: And all the multitude cried, and they said with a loud voice: As thou hast said, we will do.
text.KJV: 9:10 Then cryed the whole multitude, and sayd with a loude voice; Like as thou hast spoken, so will we doe.
text.NRSV: Then all the multitude shouted and said with a loud voice, ‘We will do as you have said.

text.DRB: But because the multitude is great, and winter time, and we cannot stand in the air without succour: and this is a work for us not of one day, nor of two, for we have sinned much in these things:
text.KJV: 9:11 But forasmuch as the people are many, and it is foule weather, so that wee cannot stand without, and this is not a worke of a day or two, seeing our sinne in these things is spread farre:
text.NRSV: But the multitude is great and it is winter, and we are not able to stand in the open air. This is not a work we can do in one day or two, for we have sinned too much in these things.

text.DRB: Let the rulers of the multitude stand, and that dwell with us, and as many as have with them foreign wives,
text.KJV: 9:12 Therefore let the rulers of the multitude stay, and let all them of our habitations that haue strange wiues, come at the time appointed,
text.NRSV: So let the leaders of the multitude stay, and let all those in our settlements who have foreign wives come at the time appointed,

text.DRB: and at a time appointed let the priests out of every place, and the judges assist, until they appease the wrath of our Lord concerning this business.
text.KJV: 9:13 And with them the Rulers and Iudges of euery place, till we turne away the wrath of the Lord from vs, for this matter.
text.NRSV: with the elders and judges of each place, until we are freed from the wrath of the Lord over this matter.’

text.DRB: And Ionathas the son of Ezeli, and Ozias of Thecam took upon them according to these words: and Bosoramus, and Levis, and Sabbathæus, wrought together with them.
text.KJV: 9:14 Then Ionathan the sonne of Azael, and Ezechias the sonne of Theocanus, accordingly tooke this matter vpon them: and Mosollam, and Leuis, and Sabbatheus helped them.
text.NRSV: Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahzeiah son of Tikvah] undertook the matter on these terms, and Meshullam and Levi and Shabbethai served with them as judges.

text.DRB: And all that were of the captivity stood according to all these things.
text.KJV: 9:15 And they that were of the captiutie, did according to all these things.
text.NRSV: And those who had returned from exile acted in accordance with all this.

text.DRB: And Esdras the priest chose unto him men the great princes of their fathers according to their names: & they sat together in the newmoon of the tenth month to examine this business.

text.NRSV: Ezra the priest chose for himself the leading men of their ancestral houses, all of them by name; and at the new moon of the tenth month they began their sessions to investigate the matter.

text.DRB: And of the children of
